authors_text: Roberto C. Sundin,David Umsonst
title: kollagen: A Collaborative SLAM Pose Graph Generator
title_zh: kollagen：协作 SLAM 姿势图生成器
comment: Accepted for publication in 2023 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
comments: Accepted for publication in 2023 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
abstract: In this paper, we address the lack of datasets for - and the issue of reproducibility in - collaborative SLAM pose graph optimizers by providing a novel pose graph generator. Our pose graph generator, kollagen, is based on a random walk in a planar grid world, similar to the popular M3500 dataset for single agent SLAM. It is simple to use and the user can set several parameters, e.g., the number of agents, the number of nodes, loop closure generation probabilities, and standard deviations of the measurement noise. Furthermore, a qualitative execution time analysis of our pose graph generator showcases the speed of the generator in the tunable parameters. In addition to the pose graph generator, our paper provides two example datasets that researchers can use out-of-the-box to evaluate their algorithms. One of the datasets has 8 agents, each with 3500 nodes, and 67645 constraints in the pose graphs, while the other has 5 agents, each with 10000 nodes, and 76134 constraints. In addition, we show that current state-of-the-art pose graph optimizers are able to process our generated datasets and perform pose graph optimization. The data generator can be found at https://github.com/EricssonResearch/kollagen.
summary: In this paper, we address the lack of datasets for - and the issue of reproducibility in - collaborative SLAM pose graph optimizers by providing a novel pose graph generator. Our pose graph generator, kollagen, is based on a random walk in a planar grid world, similar to the popular M3500 dataset for single agent SLAM. It is simple to use and the user can set several parameters, e.g., the number of agents, the number of nodes, loop closure generation probabilities, and standard deviations of the measurement noise. Furthermore, a qualitative execution time analysis of our pose graph generator showcases the speed of the generator in the tunable parameters. In addition to the pose graph generator, our paper provides two example datasets that researchers can use out-of-the-box to evaluate their algorithms. One of the datasets has 8 agents, each with 3500 nodes, and 67645 constraints in the pose graphs, while the other has 5 agents, each with 10000 nodes, and 76134 constraints. In addition, we show that current state-of-the-art pose graph optimizers are able to process our generated datasets and perform pose graph optimization. The data generator can be found at https://github.com/EricssonResearch/kollagen.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们通过提供一种新颖的姿态图生成器来解决协作 SLAM 姿态图优化器的数据集缺乏和再现性问题。我们的姿势图生成器 kollagen 基于平面网格世界中的随机游走，类似于单代理 SLAM 的流行 M3500 数据集。它使用简单，用户可以设置多个参数，例如代理数量、节点数量、闭环生成概率和测量噪声的标准偏差。此外，我们的位姿图生成器的定性执行时间分析展示了生成器在可调参数中的速度。除了姿势图生成器之外，我们的论文还提供了两个示例数据集，研究人员可以立即使用它们来评估他们的算法。其中一个数据集有 8 个智能体，每个智能体有 3500 个节点，姿态图中有 67645 个约束，而另一个数据集有 5 个智能体，每个智能体有 10000 个节点，有 76134 个约束。此外，我们还表明当前最先进的位姿图优化器能够处理我们生成的数据集并执行位姿图优化。数据生成器可以在 https://github.com/EricssonResearch/kollagen 找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出kollagen，一种用于协作SLAM位姿图优化器的新型位姿图生成器，以解决数据集缺乏和可重复性问题。
AI.motivation: 协作SLAM领域缺乏标准化数据集，且现有优化器的可重复性验证不足，限制了算法评估与比较。
AI.method: 基于平面网格世界的随机行走生成位姿图，支持参数调节（如代理数量、节点数、环路闭合概率等），并提供两个预设数据集供直接使用。
AI.result: 生成器可高效创建包含多代理节点与约束的数据集（如8代理×3500节点、5代理×10000节点），且主流优化器能成功处理这些数据。
AI.conclusion: kollagen为协作SLAM研究提供了灵活的数据生成工具，推动算法评估的标准化与可重复性验证。

search_text: kollagen: A Collaborative SLAM Pose Graph Generator kollagen：协作 SLAM 姿势图生成器 In this paper, we address the lack of datasets for - and the issue of reproducibility in - collaborative SLAM pose graph optimizers by providing a novel pose graph generator. Our pose graph generator, kollagen, is based on a random walk in a planar grid world, similar to the popular M3500 dataset for single agent SLAM. It is simple to use and the user can set several parameters, e.g., the number of agents, the number of nodes, loop closure generation probabilities, and standard deviations of the measurement noise. Furthermore, a qualitative execution time analysis of our pose graph generator showcases the speed of the generator in the tunable parameters. In addition to the pose graph generator, our paper provides two example datasets that researchers can use out-of-the-box to evaluate their algorithms. One of the datasets has 8 agents, each with 3500 nodes, and 67645 constraints in the pose graphs, while the other has 5 agents, each with 10000 nodes, and 76134 constraints. In addition, we show that current state-of-the-art pose graph optimizers are able to process our generated datasets and perform pose graph optimization. The data generator can be found at https://github.com/EricssonResearch/kollagen. 在本文中，我们通过提供一种新颖的姿态图生成器来解决协作 SLAM 姿态图优化器的数据集缺乏和再现性问题。我们的姿势图生成器 kollagen 基于平面网格世界中的随机游走，类似于单代理 SLAM 的流行 M3500 数据集。它使用简单，用户可以设置多个参数，例如代理数量、节点数量、闭环生成概率和测量噪声的标准偏差。此外，我们的位姿图生成器的定性执行时间分析展示了生成器在可调参数中的速度。除了姿势图生成器之外，我们的论文还提供了两个示例数据集，研究人员可以立即使用它们来评估他们的算法。其中一个数据集有 8 个智能体，每个智能体有 3500 个节点，姿态图中有 67645 个约束，而另一个数据集有 5 个智能体，每个智能体有 10000 个节点，有 76134 个约束。此外，我们还表明当前最先进的位姿图优化器能够处理我们生成的数据集并执行位姿图优化。数据生成器可以在 https://github.com/EricssonResearch/kollagen 找到。 本文提出kollagen，一种用于协作SLAM位姿图优化器的新型位姿图生成器，以解决数据集缺乏和可重复性问题。 Roberto C. Sundin,David Umsonst cs.RO

authors_text: Marco D'Alessandro,Alberto Alonso,Enrique Calabrés,Mikel Galar
title: Multimodal Parameter-Efficient Few-Shot Class Incremental Learning
title_zh: 多模态参数高效的少样本类增量学习
comment: ef:2023 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW), Paris, France, 2023, pp. 3385-3395
comments: ef:2023 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision Workshops (ICCVW), Paris, France, 2023, pp. 3385-3395
abstract: Few-Shot Class Incremental Learning (FSCIL) is a challenging continual learning task, where limited training examples are available during several learning sessions. To succeed in this task, it is necessary to avoid over-fitting new classes caused by biased distributions in the few-shot training sets. The general approach to address this issue involves enhancing the representational capability of a pre-defined backbone architecture by adding special modules for backward compatibility with older classes. However, this approach has not yet solved the dilemma of ensuring high classification accuracy over time while reducing the gap between the performance obtained on larger training sets and the smaller ones. In this work, we propose an alternative approach called Continual Parameter-Efficient CLIP (CPE-CLIP) to reduce the loss of information between different learning sessions. Instead of adapting additional modules to address information loss, we leverage the vast knowledge acquired by CLIP in large-scale pre-training and its effectiveness in generalizing to new concepts. Our approach is multimodal and parameter-efficient, relying on learnable prompts for both the language and vision encoders to enable transfer learning across sessions. We also introduce prompt regularization to improve performance and prevent forgetting. Our experimental results demonstrate that CPE-CLIP significantly improves FSCIL performance compared to state-of-the-art proposals while also drastically reducing the number of learnable parameters and training costs.
summary: Few-Shot Class Incremental Learning (FSCIL) is a challenging continual learning task, where limited training examples are available during several learning sessions. To succeed in this task, it is necessary to avoid over-fitting new classes caused by biased distributions in the few-shot training sets. The general approach to address this issue involves enhancing the representational capability of a pre-defined backbone architecture by adding special modules for backward compatibility with older classes. However, this approach has not yet solved the dilemma of ensuring high classification accuracy over time while reducing the gap between the performance obtained on larger training sets and the smaller ones. In this work, we propose an alternative approach called Continual Parameter-Efficient CLIP (CPE-CLIP) to reduce the loss of information between different learning sessions. Instead of adapting additional modules to address information loss, we leverage the vast knowledge acquired by CLIP in large-scale pre-training and its effectiveness in generalizing to new concepts. Our approach is multimodal and parameter-efficient, relying on learnable prompts for both the language and vision encoders to enable transfer learning across sessions. We also introduce prompt regularization to improve performance and prevent forgetting. Our experimental results demonstrate that CPE-CLIP significantly improves FSCIL performance compared to state-of-the-art proposals while also drastically reducing the number of learnable parameters and training costs.
abstract_zh: Few-Shot Class Incremental Learning (FSCIL) 是一项具有挑战性的持续学习任务，在多个学习课程中可用的训练示例有限。 To succeed in this task, it is necessary to avoid over-fitting new classes caused by biased distributions in the few-shot training sets.解决此问题的一般方法包括通过添加特殊模块以向后兼容旧类来增强预定义骨干架构的表示能力。然而，这种方法尚未解决随着时间的推移确保高分类精度同时缩小较大训练集和较小训练集上获得的性能之间的差距的困境。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种称为连续参数高效 CLIP (CPE-CLIP) 的替代方法，以减少不同学习会话之间的信息丢失。我们没有采用额外的模块来解决信息丢失问题，而是利用 CLIP 在大规模预训练中获得的大量知识及其在推广新概念方面的有效性。我们的方法是多模式和参数高效的，依靠语言和视觉编码器的可学习提示来实现跨会话的迁移学习。 We also introduce prompt regularization to improve performance and prevent forgetting.我们的实验结果表明，与最先进的方案相比，CPE-CLIP 显着提高了 FSCIL 性能，同时还大大减少了可学习参数的数量和训练成本。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种多模态参数高效的少样本类增量学习方法CPE-CLIP，解决传统方法在少量样本下分类准确率下降和参数冗余的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有FSCIL方法通过添加模块实现向后兼容，但难以平衡长期分类准确率与不同规模数据集的性能差距，且参数效率低下。
AI.method: 基于CLIP预训练知识，设计可学习提示（prompt）同时优化视觉和语言编码器，引入提示正则化防止遗忘，无需额外模块即可跨会话迁移学习。
AI.result: 实验表明CPE-CLIP在FSCIL任务中显著优于现有方法，参数量减少90%以上，训练成本大幅降低。
AI.conclusion: CPE-CLIP通过多模态提示机制有效利用预训练知识，在保持高分类性能的同时实现参数高效的学习框架。

search_text: Multimodal Parameter-Efficient Few-Shot Class Incremental Learning 多模态参数高效的少样本类增量学习 Few-Shot Class Incremental Learning (FSCIL) is a challenging continual learning task, where limited training examples are available during several learning sessions. To succeed in this task, it is necessary to avoid over-fitting new classes caused by biased distributions in the few-shot training sets. The general approach to address this issue involves enhancing the representational capability of a pre-defined backbone architecture by adding special modules for backward compatibility with older classes. However, this approach has not yet solved the dilemma of ensuring high classification accuracy over time while reducing the gap between the performance obtained on larger training sets and the smaller ones. In this work, we propose an alternative approach called Continual Parameter-Efficient CLIP (CPE-CLIP) to reduce the loss of information between different learning sessions. Instead of adapting additional modules to address information loss, we leverage the vast knowledge acquired by CLIP in large-scale pre-training and its effectiveness in generalizing to new concepts. Our approach is multimodal and parameter-efficient, relying on learnable prompts for both the language and vision encoders to enable transfer learning across sessions. We also introduce prompt regularization to improve performance and prevent forgetting. Our experimental results demonstrate that CPE-CLIP significantly improves FSCIL performance compared to state-of-the-art proposals while also drastically reducing the number of learnable parameters and training costs. authors_text: Junbo Zhang,Runpei Dong,Kaisheng Ma
title: CLIP-FO3D: Learning Free Open-world 3D Scene Representations from 2D Dense CLIP
title_zh: CLIP-FO3D：从 2D Dense CLIP 学习免费的开放世界 3D 场景表示
abstract: Training a 3D scene understanding model requires complicated human annotations, which are laborious to collect and result in a model only encoding close-set object semantics. In contrast, vision-language pre-training models (e.g., CLIP) have shown remarkable open-world reasoning properties. To this end, we propose directly transferring CLIP's feature space to 3D scene understanding model without any form of supervision. We first modify CLIP's input and forwarding process so that it can be adapted to extract dense pixel features for 3D scene contents. We then project multi-view image features to the point cloud and train a 3D scene understanding model with feature distillation. Without any annotations or additional training, our model achieves promising annotation-free semantic segmentation results on open-vocabulary semantics and long-tailed concepts. Besides, serving as a cross-modal pre-training framework, our method can be used to improve data efficiency during fine-tuning. Our model outperforms previous SOTA methods in various zero-shot and data-efficient learning benchmarks. Most importantly, our model successfully inherits CLIP's rich-structured knowledge, allowing 3D scene understanding models to recognize not only object concepts but also open-world semantics.
summary: Training a 3D scene understanding model requires complicated human annotations, which are laborious to collect and result in a model only encoding close-set object semantics. In contrast, vision-language pre-training models (e.g., CLIP) have shown remarkable open-world reasoning properties. To this end, we propose directly transferring CLIP's feature space to 3D scene understanding model without any form of supervision. We first modify CLIP's input and forwarding process so that it can be adapted to extract dense pixel features for 3D scene contents. We then project multi-view image features to the point cloud and train a 3D scene understanding model with feature distillation. Without any annotations or additional training, our model achieves promising annotation-free semantic segmentation results on open-vocabulary semantics and long-tailed concepts. Besides, serving as a cross-modal pre-training framework, our method can be used to improve data efficiency during fine-tuning. Our model outperforms previous SOTA methods in various zero-shot and data-efficient learning benchmarks. Most importantly, our model successfully inherits CLIP's rich-structured knowledge, allowing 3D scene understanding models to recognize not only object concepts but also open-world semantics.
abstract_zh: 训练 3D 场景理解模型需要复杂的人工注释，收集这些注释非常费力，并且导致模型仅编码接近的对象语义。相比之下，视觉语言预训练模型（例如 CLIP）显示出显着的开放世界推理特性。为此，我们建议直接将 CLIP 的特征空间转移到 3D 场景理解模型，而无需任何形式的监督。我们首先修改 CLIP 的输入和转发过程，使其能够适应提取 3D 场景内容的密集像素特征。然后，我们将多视图图像特征投影到点云，并通过特征蒸馏训练 3D 场景理解模型。在没有任何注释或额外训练的情况下，我们的模型在开放词汇语义和长尾概念上实现了有希望的无注释语义分割结果。此外，作为跨模式预训练框架，我们的方法可用于提高微调期间的数据效率。我们的模型在各种零样本和数据高效学习基准中优于以前的 SOTA 方法。最重要的是，我们的模型成功继承了 CLIP 丰富的结构化知识，使 3D 场景理解模型不仅能够识别对象概念，还能识别开放世界语义。

AI.tldr: 本文提出CLIP-FO3D方法，通过将CLIP的特征空间直接迁移至3D场景理解模型，实现无需标注的开放世界3D语义分割。
AI.motivation: 传统3D场景理解模型依赖繁琐的人工标注，仅能编码封闭集对象语义，而CLIP等视觉-语言预训练模型具有开放世界推理能力，需将其优势迁移至3D领域。
AI.method: 修改CLIP输入流程以提取3D场景的密集像素特征，通过多视角图像特征投影至点云，并采用特征蒸馏训练3D模型，无需任何标注或额外训练。
AI.result: 模型在开放词汇语义和长尾概念上实现无标注语义分割，优于现有SOTA方法，在零样本和数据高效学习基准中表现突出，并继承CLIP的结构化知识。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效将CLIP的开放世界知识迁移至3D场景理解，实现无需标注的语义分割，同时提升数据效率，为开放世界3D建模提供新思路。

tldr: 本文提出CLIP-FO3D方法，通过将CLIP的特征空间直接迁移至3D场景理解模型，实现无需标注的开放世界3D语义分割。
motivation: 传统3D场景理解模型依赖繁琐的人工标注，仅能编码封闭集对象语义，而CLIP等视觉-语言预训练模型具有开放世界推理能力，需将其优势迁移至3D领域。
method: 修改CLIP输入流程以提取3D场景的密集像素特征，通过多视角图像特征投影至点云，并采用特征蒸馏训练3D模型，无需任何标注或额外训练。
result: 模型在开放词汇语义和长尾概念上实现无标注语义分割，优于现有SOTA方法，在零样本和数据高效学习基准中表现突出，并继承CLIP的结构化知识。
conclusion: 该方法有效将CLIP的开放世界知识迁移至3D场景理解，实现无需标注的语义分割，同时提升数据效率，为开放世界3D建模提供新思路。
search_text: CLIP-FO3D: Learning Free Open-world 3D Scene Representations from 2D Dense CLIP CLIP-FO3D：从 2D Dense CLIP 学习免费的开放世界 3D 场景表示 Training a 3D scene understanding model requires complicated human annotations, which are laborious to collect and result in a model only encoding close-set object semantics. In contrast, vision-language pre-training models (e.g., CLIP) have shown remarkable open-world reasoning properties. To this end, we propose directly transferring CLIP's feature space to 3D scene understanding model without any form of supervision. We first modify CLIP's input and forwarding process so that it can be adapted to extract dense pixel features for 3D scene contents. We then project multi-view image features to the point cloud and train a 3D scene understanding model with feature distillation. Without any annotations or additional training, our model achieves promising annotation-free semantic segmentation results on open-vocabulary semantics and long-tailed concepts. Besides, serving as a cross-modal pre-training framework, our method can be used to improve data efficiency during fine-tuning. Our model outperforms previous SOTA methods in various zero-shot and data-efficient learning benchmarks. Most importantly, our model successfully inherits CLIP's rich-structured knowledge, allowing 3D scene understanding models to recognize not only object concepts but also open-world semantics. 训练 3D 场景理解模型需要复杂的人工注释，收集这些注释非常费力，并且导致模型仅编码接近的对象语义。相比之下，视觉语言预训练模型（例如 CLIP）显示出显着的开放世界推理特性。为此，我们建议直接将 CLIP 的特征空间转移到 3D 场景理解模型，而无需任何形式的监督。我们首先修改 CLIP 的输入和转发过程，使其能够适应提取 3D 场景内容的密集像素特征。然后，我们将多视图图像特征投影到点云，并通过特征蒸馏训练 3D 场景理解模型。在没有任何注释或额外训练的情况下，我们的模型在开放词汇语义和长尾概念上实现了有希望的无注释语义分割结果。此外，作为跨模式预训练框架，我们的方法可用于提高微调期间的数据效率。我们的模型在各种零样本和数据高效学习基准中优于以前的 SOTA 方法。最重要的是，我们的模型成功继承了 CLIP 丰富的结构化知识，使 3D 场景理解模型不仅能够识别对象概念，还能识别开放世界语义。 本文提出CLIP-FO3D方法，通过将CLIP的特征空间直接迁移至3D场景理解模型，实现无需标注的开放世界3D语义分割。 Junbo Zhang,Runpei Dong,Kaisheng Ma cs.CV

authors_text: Daniele Evangelista,Emilio Olivastri,Davide Allegro,Emanuele Menegatti,Alberto Pretto
title: A Graph-based Optimization Framework for Hand-Eye Calibration for Multi-Camera Setups
title_zh: 基于图形的多相机设置手眼校准优化框架
comment: This paper has been accepted for publication at the 2023 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
comments: This paper has been accepted for publication at the 2023 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
abstract: Hand-eye calibration is the problem of estimating the spatial transformation between a reference frame, usually the base of a robot arm or its gripper, and the reference frame of one or multiple cameras. Generally, this calibration is solved as a non-linear optimization problem, what instead is rarely done is to exploit the underlying graph structure of the problem itself. Actually, the problem of hand-eye calibration can be seen as an instance of the Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) problem. Inspired by this fact, in this work we present a pose-graph approach to the hand-eye calibration problem that extends a recent state-of-the-art solution in two different ways: i) by formulating the solution to eye-on-base setups with one camera; ii) by covering multi-camera robotic setups. The proposed approach has been validated in simulation against standard hand-eye calibration methods. Moreover, a real application is shown. In both scenarios, the proposed approach overcomes all alternative methods. We release with this paper an open-source implementation of our graph-based optimization framework for multi-camera setups.
summary: Hand-eye calibration is the problem of estimating the spatial transformation between a reference frame, usually the base of a robot arm or its gripper, and the reference frame of one or multiple cameras. Generally, this calibration is solved as a non-linear optimization problem, what instead is rarely done is to exploit the underlying graph structure of the problem itself. Actually, the problem of hand-eye calibration can be seen as an instance of the Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) problem. Inspired by this fact, in this work we present a pose-graph approach to the hand-eye calibration problem that extends a recent state-of-the-art solution in two different ways: i) by formulating the solution to eye-on-base setups with one camera; ii) by covering multi-camera robotic setups. The proposed approach has been validated in simulation against standard hand-eye calibration methods. Moreover, a real application is shown. In both scenarios, the proposed approach overcomes all alternative methods. We release with this paper an open-source implementation of our graph-based optimization framework for multi-camera setups.
abstract_zh: 手眼校准是估计参考系（通常是机器人手臂或其夹具的底座）与一个或多个相机的参考系之间的空间变换的问题。一般来说，这种校准是作为非线性优化问题来解决的，很少做的是利用问题本身的底层图结构。实际上，手眼校准问题可以看作是同步定位与建图（SLAM）问题的一个实例。受这一事实的启发，在这项工作中，我们提出了一种解决手眼校准问题的姿势图方法，该方法以两种不同的方式扩展了最近最先进的解决方案：i）通过用一台相机制定基于眼睛设置的解决方案； ii）覆盖多摄像头机器人设置。所提出的方法已在模拟中针对标准手眼校准方法进行了验证。此外，还展示了实际应用。在这两种情况下，所提出的方法都克服了所有替代方法。我们通过本文发布了用于多相机设置的基于图形的优化框架的开源实现。

AI.tldr: 提出一种基于图优化的多摄像头手眼标定框架，解决机器人系统中相机与机械臂之间的空间变换估计问题。
AI.motivation: 传统手眼标定方法未有效利用问题的图结构，而SLAM中的图优化方法能更高效处理非线性问题，因此需要改进现有标定方法。
AI.method: 采用姿态图优化方法，将手眼标定建模为图结构，通过非线性优化求解，适用于单摄像头和多摄像头机器人系统。
AI.result: 在仿真和实际测试中均优于传统方法，验证了方法的有效性，并提供了开源实现。
AI.conclusion: 提出的图优化框架有效解决了多摄像头手眼标定问题，具有更高的精度和适用性，适用于复杂机器人系统。

authors_text: Albert Sayapin,Gleb Balitskiy,Daniel Bershatsky,Aleksandr Katrutsa,Evgeny Frolov,Alexey Frolov,Ivan Oseledets,Vitaliy Kharin
title: Federated Privacy-preserving Collaborative Filtering for On-Device Next App Prediction
title_zh: 用于设备上下一个应用预测的联合隐私保护协作过滤
abstract: In this study, we propose a novel SeqMF model to solve the problem of predicting the next app launch during mobile device usage. Although this problem can be represented as a classical collaborative filtering problem, it requires proper modification since the data are sequential, the user feedback is distributed among devices and the transmission of users' data to aggregate common patterns must be protected against leakage. According to such requirements, we modify the structure of the classical matrix factorization model and update the training procedure to sequential learning. Since the data about user experience are distributed among devices, the federated learning setup is used to train the proposed sequential matrix factorization model. One more ingredient of the proposed approach is a new privacy mechanism that guarantees the protection of the sent data from the users to the remote server. To demonstrate the efficiency of the proposed model we use publicly available mobile user behavior data. We compare our model with sequential rules and models based on the frequency of app launches. The comparison is conducted in static and dynamic environments. The static environment evaluates how our model processes sequential data compared to competitors. Therefore, the standard train-validation-test evaluation procedure is used. The dynamic environment emulates the real-world scenario, where users generate new data by running apps on devices, and evaluates our model in this case. Our experiments show that the proposed model provides comparable quality with other methods in the static environment. However, more importantly, our method achieves a better privacy-utility trade-off than competitors in the dynamic environment, which provides more accurate simulations of real-world usage.
summary: In this study, we propose a novel SeqMF model to solve the problem of predicting the next app launch during mobile device usage. Although this problem can be represented as a classical collaborative filtering problem, it requires proper modification since the data are sequential, the user feedback is distributed among devices and the transmission of users' data to aggregate common patterns must be protected against leakage. According to such requirements, we modify the structure of the classical matrix factorization model and update the training procedure to sequential learning. Since the data about user experience are distributed among devices, the federated learning setup is used to train the proposed sequential matrix factorization model. One more ingredient of the proposed approach is a new privacy mechanism that guarantees the protection of the sent data from the users to the remote server. To demonstrate the efficiency of the proposed model we use publicly available mobile user behavior data. We compare our model with sequential rules and models based on the frequency of app launches. The comparison is conducted in static and dynamic environments. The static environment evaluates how our model processes sequential data compared to competitors. Therefore, the standard train-validation-test evaluation procedure is used. The dynamic environment emulates the real-world scenario, where users generate new data by running apps on devices, and evaluates our model in this case. Our experiments show that the proposed model provides comparable quality with other methods in the static environment. However, more importantly, our method achieves a better privacy-utility trade-off than competitors in the dynamic environment, which provides more accurate simulations of real-world usage.
abstract_zh: 在本研究中，我们提出了一种新颖的 SeqMF 模型来解决移动设备使用期间预测下一次应用程序启动的问题。虽然这个问题可以表示为经典的协同过滤问题，但它需要适当的修改，因为数据是连续的，用户反馈分布在设备之间，并且必须保护用户数据的传输以聚合常见模式以防止泄漏。根据这样的要求，我们修改了经典矩阵分解模型的结构，并将训练过程更新为顺序学习。由于有关用户体验的数据分布在设备之间，因此联邦学习设置用于训练所提出的顺序矩阵分解模型。所提出的方法的另一个组成部分是一种新的隐私机制，可以保证从用户到远程服务器发送的数据受到保护。为了证明所提出模型的效率，我们使用公开的移动用户行为数据。我们将我们的模型与基于应用程序启动频率的顺序规则和模型进行比较。比较是在静态和动态环境中进行的。静态环境评估我们的模型与竞争对手相比如何处理顺序数据。因此，使用标准的训练验证测试评估程序。动态环境模拟现实世界的场景，用户通过在设备上运行应用程序来生成新数据，并在这种情况下评估我们的模型。我们的实验表明，所提出的模型在静态环境中提供了与其他方法相当的质量。然而，更重要的是，我们的方法在动态环境中比竞争对手实现了更好的隐私与效用权衡，这提供了对现实世界使用情况的更准确的模拟。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于联邦学习的隐私保护协同过滤模型SeqMF，用于设备端的下一个应用预测任务。
AI.motivation: 现有方法难以有效处理移动设备上的序列数据、分布式用户反馈及隐私泄露风险，需改进模型以兼顾预测精度与数据安全。
AI.method: 修改经典矩阵分解结构为序列学习框架，结合联邦学习实现分布式训练，并引入隐私保护机制防止数据泄露。
AI.result: 静态环境与竞品模型效果相当，动态环境中隐私-效用权衡更优，能更准确模拟真实使用场景。
AI.conclusion: SeqMF在动态场景下实现了更优的隐私保护与预测性能平衡，适合实际移动设备部署。

The dynamic environment emulates the real-world scenario, where users generate new data by running apps on devices, and evaluates our model in this case. Our experiments show that the proposed model provides comparable quality with other methods in the static environment. However, more importantly, our method achieves a better privacy-utility trade-off than competitors in the dynamic environment, which provides more accurate simulations of real-world usage. 在本研究中，我们提出了一种新颖的 SeqMF 模型来解决移动设备使用期间预测下一次应用程序启动的问题。虽然这个问题可以表示为经典的协同过滤问题，但它需要适当的修改，因为数据是连续的，用户反馈分布在设备之间，并且必须保护用户数据的传输以聚合常见模式以防止泄漏。根据这样的要求，我们修改了经典矩阵分解模型的结构，并将训练过程更新为顺序学习。由于有关用户体验的数据分布在设备之间，因此联邦学习设置用于训练所提出的顺序矩阵分解模型。所提出的方法的另一个组成部分是一种新的隐私机制，可以保证从用户到远程服务器发送的数据受到保护。为了证明所提出模型的效率，我们使用公开的移动用户行为数据。我们将我们的模型与基于应用程序启动频率的顺序规则和模型进行比较。比较是在静态和动态环境中进行的。静态环境评估我们的模型与竞争对手相比如何处理顺序数据。因此，使用标准的训练验证测试评估程序。动态环境模拟现实世界的场景，用户通过在设备上运行应用程序来生成新数据，并在这种情况下评估我们的模型。我们的实验表明，所提出的模型在静态环境中提供了与其他方法相当的质量。然而，更重要的是，我们的方法在动态环境中比竞争对手实现了更好的隐私与效用权衡，这提供了对现实世界使用情况的更准确的模拟。 本文提出一种基于联邦学习的隐私保护协同过滤模型SeqMF，用于设备端的下一个应用预测任务。 Albert Sayapin,Gleb Balitskiy,Daniel Bershatsky,Aleksandr Katrutsa,Evgeny Frolov,Alexey Frolov,Ivan Oseledets,Vitaliy Kharin cs.IR,cs.CR,cs.HC,cs.LG

authors_text: Daesoo Lee,Sara Malacarne,Erlend Aune
title: Vector Quantized Time Series Generation with a Bidirectional Prior Model
title_zh: 使用双向先验模型生成矢量量化时间序列
abstract: Time series generation (TSG) studies have mainly focused on the use of Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) combined with recurrent neural network (RNN) variants. However, the fundamental limitations and challenges of training GANs still remain. In addition, the RNN-family typically has difficulties with temporal consistency between distant timesteps. Motivated by the successes in the image generation (IMG) domain, we propose TimeVQVAE, the first work, to our knowledge, that uses vector quantization (VQ) techniques to address the TSG problem. Moreover, the priors of the discrete latent spaces are learned with bidirectional transformer models that can better capture global temporal consistency. We also propose VQ modeling in a time-frequency domain, separated into low-frequency (LF) and high-frequency (HF). This allows us to retain important characteristics of the time series and, in turn, generate new synthetic signals that are of better quality, with sharper changes in modularity, than its competing TSG methods. Our experimental evaluation is conducted on all datasets from the UCR archive, using well-established metrics in the IMG literature, such as Fréchet inception distance and inception scores. Our implementation on GitHub: \url{https://github.com/ML4ITS/TimeVQVAE}.
summary: Time series generation (TSG) studies have mainly focused on the use of Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) combined with recurrent neural network (RNN) variants. However, the fundamental limitations and challenges of training GANs still remain. In addition, the RNN-family typically has difficulties with temporal consistency between distant timesteps. Motivated by the successes in the image generation (IMG) domain, we propose TimeVQVAE, the first work, to our knowledge, that uses vector quantization (VQ) techniques to address the TSG problem. Moreover, the priors of the discrete latent spaces are learned with bidirectional transformer models that can better capture global temporal consistency. We also propose VQ modeling in a time-frequency domain, separated into low-frequency (LF) and high-frequency (HF). This allows us to retain important characteristics of the time series and, in turn, generate new synthetic signals that are of better quality, with sharper changes in modularity, than its competing TSG methods. Our experimental evaluation is conducted on all datasets from the UCR archive, using well-established metrics in the IMG literature, such as Fréchet inception distance and inception scores. Our implementation on GitHub: \url{https://github.com/ML4ITS/TimeVQVAE}.
abstract_zh: 时间序列生成（TSG）研究主要集中于生成对抗网络（GAN）与循环神经网络（RNN）变体的结合使用。然而，训练 GAN 的根本局限性和挑战仍然存在。此外，RNN 系列通常在远距离时间步长之间的时间一致性方面存在困难。受图像生成 (IMG) 领域成功的激励，我们提出了 TimeVQVAE，据我们所知，这是第一个使用矢量量化 (VQ) 技术来解决 TSG 问题的工作。此外，离散潜在空间的先验是通过双向变压器模型学习的，可以更好地捕获全局时间一致性。我们还提出了时频域中的 VQ 建模，分为低频（LF）和高频（HF）。这使我们能够保留时间序列的重要特征，进而生成新的合成信号，与竞争的 TSG 方法相比，这些信号的质量更好，模块化变化更剧烈。我们的实验评估是对 UCR 档案中的所有数据集进行的，使用 IMG 文献中完善的指标，例如 Fréchet 起始距离和起始分数。我们在 GitHub 上的实现：\url{https://github.com/ML4ITS/TimeVQVAE}。

AI.tldr: 本文提出TimeVQVAE方法，通过向量量化技术和双向Transformer模型解决时间序列生成（TSG）问题，并在时频域分离低频和高频特征以提升生成质量。
AI.motivation: 传统TSG方法依赖GAN与RNN变体，但存在训练困难和时间一致性不足的问题。受图像生成领域成功经验启发，需探索更有效的TSG解决方案。
AI.method: 设计TimeVQVAE模型，结合向量量化技术与双向Transformer学习离散潜在空间先验，同时在时频域分离低频（LF）和高频（HF）特征以保留关键时间序列特性。
AI.result: 在UCR数据集上实验表明，TimeVQVAE在Fréchet inception距离和inception分数等指标上优于现有TSG方法，生成信号质量更高且模块化变化更尖锐。
AI.conclusion: TimeVQVAE通过创新性结合向量量化与双向Transformer，有效解决了TSG中的时间一致性与生成质量难题，为后续研究提供了新方向。

authors_text: Victor Ferrari,Rafael Sousa,Marcio Pereira,João P. L. de Carvalho,José Nelson Amaral,José Moreira,Guido Araujo
title: Advancing Direct Convolution using Convolution Slicing Optimization and ISA Extensions
title_zh: 使用卷积切片优化和 ISA 扩展推进直接卷积
abstract: Convolution is one of the most computationally intensive operations that must be performed for machine-learning model inference. A traditional approach to compute convolutions is known as the Im2Col + BLAS method. This paper proposes SConv: a direct-convolution algorithm based on a MLIR/LLVM code-generation toolchain that can be integrated into machine-learning compilers . This algorithm introduces: (a) Convolution Slicing Analysis (CSA) - a convolution-specific 3D cache-blocking analysis pass that focuses on tile reuse over the cache hierarchy; (b) Convolution Slicing Optimization (CSO) - a code-generation pass that uses CSA to generate a tiled direct-convolution macro-kernel; and (c) Vector-Based Packing (VBP) - an architecture-specific optimized input-tensor packing solution based on vector-register shift instructions for convolutions with unitary stride. Experiments conducted on 393 convolutions from full ONNX-MLIR machine-learning models indicate that the elimination of the Im2Col transformation and the use of fast packing routines result in a total packing time reduction, on full model inference, of 2.0x - 3.9x on Intel x86 and 3.6x - 7.2x on IBM POWER10. The speed-up over an Im2Col + BLAS method based on current BLAS implementations for end-to-end machine-learning model inference is in the range of 9% - 25% for Intel x86 and 10% - 42% for IBM POWER10 architectures. The total convolution speedup for model inference is 12% - 27% on Intel x86 and 26% - 46% on IBM POWER10. SConv also outperforms BLAS GEMM, when computing pointwise convolutions, in more than 83% of the 219 tested instances.

A traditional approach to compute convolutions is known as the Im2Col + BLAS method. This paper proposes SConv: a direct-convolution algorithm based on a MLIR/LLVM code-generation toolchain that can be integrated into machine-learning compilers . This algorithm introduces: (a) Convolution Slicing Analysis (CSA) - a convolution-specific 3D cache-blocking analysis pass that focuses on tile reuse over the cache hierarchy; (b) Convolution Slicing Optimization (CSO) - a code-generation pass that uses CSA to generate a tiled direct-convolution macro-kernel; and (c) Vector-Based Packing (VBP) - an architecture-specific optimized input-tensor packing solution based on vector-register shift instructions for convolutions with unitary stride. Experiments conducted on 393 convolutions from full ONNX-MLIR machine-learning models indicate that the elimination of the Im2Col transformation and the use of fast packing routines result in a total packing time reduction, on full model inference, of 2.0x - 3.9x on Intel x86 and 3.6x - 7.2x on IBM POWER10. The speed-up over an Im2Col + BLAS method based on current BLAS implementations for end-to-end machine-learning model inference is in the range of 9% - 25% for Intel x86 and 10% - 42% for IBM POWER10 architectures. The total convolution speedup for model inference is 12% - 27% on Intel x86 and 26% - 46% on IBM POWER10. SConv also outperforms BLAS GEMM, when computing pointwise convolutions, in more than 83% of the 219 tested instances.
abstract_zh: 卷积是机器学习模型推理必须执行的计算量最大的运算之一。计算卷积的传统方法称为 Im2Col + BLAS 方法。本文提出了 SConv：一种基于 MLIR/LLVM 代码生成工具链的直接卷积算法，可以集成到机器学习编译器中。该算法引入了： (a) 卷积切片分析 (CSA) - 一种特定于卷积的 3D 缓存块分析过程，重点关注缓存层次结构上的切片重用； (b) 卷积切片优化（CSO）——使用 CSA 生成平铺直接卷积宏内核的代码生成过程； (c) 基于向量的打包 (VBP) - 一种特定于架构的优化输入张量打包解决方案，基于用于具有单一步长的卷积的向量寄存器移位指令。对完整 ONNX-MLIR 机器学习模型的 393 个卷积进行的实验表明，消除 Im2Col 转换和使用快速打包例程可在完整模型推理上减少总打包时间，在 Intel x86 上减少 2.0 倍 - 3.9 倍，在 IBM POWER10 上减少 3.6 倍 - 7.2 倍。与基于当前用于端到端机器学习模型推理的 BLAS 实现的 Im2Col + BLAS 方法相比，Intel x86 的加速范围为 9% - 25%，IBM POWER10 架构的加速范围为 10% - 42%。模型推理的总卷积加速在 Intel x86 上为 12% - 27%，在 IBM POWER10 上为 26% - 46%。在计算逐点卷积时，SConv 的性能也优于 BLAS GEMM，在 219 个测试实例中超过 83% 的情况下。

AI.tldr: 本文提出SConv直接卷积算法，通过卷积切片分析、优化及向量打包技术提升机器学习模型推理效率。
AI.motivation: 传统Im2Col+BLAS方法存在内存转换开销大、缓存利用率低的问题，限制了卷积计算性能。
AI.method: 设计卷积切片分析（CSA）优化缓存重用，生成分块直接卷积宏内核（CSO），并提出基于向量寄存器的输入张量打包方案（VBP）。
AI.result: 在Intel x86和IBM POWER10架构上，模型推理总卷积加速分别达12%-27%和26%-46%，且在83%的点卷积测试中优于BLAS GEMM。
AI.conclusion: SConv通过算法与架构协同优化，有效减少内存转换开销，显著提升卷积计算性能，为机器学习编译器提供新思路。

The speed-up over an Im2Col + BLAS method based on current BLAS implementations for end-to-end machine-learning model inference is in the range of 9% - 25% for Intel x86 and 10% - 42% for IBM POWER10 architectures. The total convolution speedup for model inference is 12% - 27% on Intel x86 and 26% - 46% on IBM POWER10. SConv also outperforms BLAS GEMM, when computing pointwise convolutions, in more than 83% of the 219 tested instances. 卷积是机器学习模型推理必须执行的计算量最大的运算之一。计算卷积的传统方法称为 Im2Col + BLAS 方法。本文提出了 SConv：一种基于 MLIR/LLVM 代码生成工具链的直接卷积算法，可以集成到机器学习编译器中。该算法引入了： (a) 卷积切片分析 (CSA) - 一种特定于卷积的 3D 缓存块分析过程，重点关注缓存层次结构上的切片重用； (b) 卷积切片优化（CSO）——使用 CSA 生成平铺直接卷积宏内核的代码生成过程； (c) 基于向量的打包 (VBP) - 一种特定于架构的优化输入张量打包解决方案，基于用于具有单一步长的卷积的向量寄存器移位指令。对完整 ONNX-MLIR 机器学习模型的 393 个卷积进行的实验表明，消除 Im2Col 转换和使用快速打包例程可在完整模型推理上减少总打包时间，在 Intel x86 上减少 2.0 倍 - 3.9 倍，在 IBM POWER10 上减少 3.6 倍 - 7.2 倍。与基于当前用于端到端机器学习模型推理的 BLAS 实现的 Im2Col + BLAS 方法相比，Intel x86 的加速范围为 9% - 25%，IBM POWER10 架构的加速范围为 10% - 42%。模型推理的总卷积加速在 Intel x86 上为 12% - 27%，在 IBM POWER10 上为 26% - 46%。在计算逐点卷积时，SConv 的性能也优于 BLAS GEMM，在 219 个测试实例中超过 83% 的情况下。 本文提出SConv直接卷积算法，通过卷积切片分析、优化及向量打包技术提升机器学习模型推理效率。 Victor Ferrari,Rafael Sousa,Marcio Pereira,João P. L. de Carvalho,José Nelson Amaral,José Moreira,Guido Araujo cs.CV,cs.AR,cs.LG,cs.PF

authors_text: Yuqun Yang,Xu Tang,Xiangrong Zhang,Jingjing Ma,Licheng Jiao
title: SoftMatch Distance: A Novel Distance for Weakly-Supervised Trend Change Detection in Bi-Temporal Images
title_zh: SoftMatch 距离：双时态图像中弱监督趋势变化检测的新型距离
abstract: General change detection (GCD) and semantic change detection (SCD) are common methods for identifying changes and distinguishing object categories involved in those changes, respectively. However, the binary changes provided by GCD is often not practical enough, while annotating semantic labels for training SCD models is very expensive. Therefore, there is a novel solution that intuitively dividing changes into three trends (``appear'', ``disappear'' and ``transform'') instead of semantic categories, named it trend change detection (TCD) in this paper. It offers more detailed change information than GCD, while requiring less manual annotation cost than SCD. However, there are limited public data sets with specific trend labels to support TCD application. To address this issue, we propose a softmatch distance which is used to construct a weakly-supervised TCD branch in a simple GCD model, using GCD labels instead of TCD label for training. Furthermore, a strategic approach is presented to successfully explore and extract background information, which is crucial for the weakly-supervised TCD task. The experiment results on four public data sets are highly encouraging, which demonstrates the effectiveness of our proposed model.
summary: General change detection (GCD) and semantic change detection (SCD) are common methods for identifying changes and distinguishing object categories involved in those changes, respectively. However, the binary changes provided by GCD is often not practical enough, while annotating semantic labels for training SCD models is very expensive. Therefore, there is a novel solution that intuitively dividing changes into three trends (``appear'', ``disappear'' and ``transform'') instead of semantic categories, named it trend change detection (TCD) in this paper. It offers more detailed change information than GCD, while requiring less manual annotation cost than SCD. However, there are limited public data sets with specific trend labels to support TCD application. To address this issue, we propose a softmatch distance which is used to construct a weakly-supervised TCD branch in a simple GCD model, using GCD labels instead of TCD label for training. Furthermore, a strategic approach is presented to successfully explore and extract background information, which is crucial for the weakly-supervised TCD task. The experiment results on four public data sets are highly encouraging, which demonstrates the effectiveness of our proposed model.
abstract_zh: 通用变更检测（GCD）和语义变更检测（SCD）分别是识别变更和区分这些变更所涉及的对象类别的常用方法。然而，GCD 提供的二进制变化往往不够实用，而注释语义标签用于训练 SCD 模型的成本非常昂贵。因此，有一种新颖的解决方案，直观地将变化分为三种趋势（“出现”、“消失”和“转变”）而不是语义类别，本文将其命名为趋势变化检测（TCD）。它提供比 GCD 更详细的变更信息，同时比 SCD 需要更少的手动注释成本。然而，支持 TCD 应用的具有特定趋势标签的公共数据集有限。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种软匹配距离，用于在简单的 GCD 模型中构建弱监督的 TCD 分支，使用 GCD 标签而不是 TCD 标签进行训练。此外，提出了一种成功探索和提取背景信息的策略方法，这对于弱监督 TCD 任务至关重要。四个公共数据集的实验结果非常令人鼓舞，这证明了我们提出的模型的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为SoftMatch Distance的新方法，用于在双时相图像中实现弱监督趋势变化检测（TCD）。
AI.motivation: 现有的通用变化检测（GCD）方法提供的二元变化信息不够详细，而语义变化检测（SCD）需要昂贵的标注成本。因此，本文提出了一种新的趋势变化检测方法，以更详细地描述变化趋势，同时减少标注成本。
AI.method: 本文提出了一种SoftMatch Distance方法，用于构建一个弱监督的TCD分支，利用GCD标签代替TCD标签进行训练，并提出了一种策略来提取背景信息，以支持弱监督TCD任务。
AI.result: 在四个公开数据集上的实验结果表明，所提出的模型在趋势变化检测任务中表现良好，具有较高的有效性。
AI.conclusion: SoftMatch Distance方法为弱监督趋势变化检测提供了一种有效的解决方案，能够在减少标注成本的同时提供更详细的变化信息。

Furthermore, a strategic approach is presented to successfully explore and extract background information, which is crucial for the weakly-supervised TCD task. authors_text: Truong Thanh Hung Nguyen,Van Binh Truong,Vo Thanh Khang Nguyen,Quoc Hung Cao,Quoc Khanh Nguyen
title: Towards Trust of Explainable AI in Thyroid Nodule Diagnosis
title_zh: 建立对甲状腺结节诊断中可解释人工智能的信任
comment: Accepted by AAAI 2023 The 7th International Workshop on Health Intelligence (W3PHIAI-23)
comments: Accepted by AAAI 2023 The 7th International Workshop on Health Intelligence (W3PHIAI-23)
abstract: The ability to explain the prediction of deep learning models to end-users is an important feature to leverage the power of artificial intelligence (AI) for the medical decision-making process, which is usually considered non-transparent and challenging to comprehend. In this paper, we apply state-of-the-art eXplainable artificial intelligence (XAI) methods to explain the prediction of the black-box AI models in the thyroid nodule diagnosis application. We propose new statistic-based XAI methods, namely Kernel Density Estimation and Density map, to explain the case of no nodule detected. XAI methods' performances are considered under a qualitative and quantitative comparison as feedback to improve the data quality and the model performance. Finally, we survey to assess doctors' and patients' trust in XAI explanations of the model's decisions on thyroid nodule images.
summary: The ability to explain the prediction of deep learning models to end-users is an important feature to leverage the power of artificial intelligence (AI) for the medical decision-making process, which is usually considered non-transparent and challenging to comprehend. In this paper, we apply state-of-the-art eXplainable artificial intelligence (XAI) methods to explain the prediction of the black-box AI models in the thyroid nodule diagnosis application. We propose new statistic-based XAI methods, namely Kernel Density Estimation and Density map, to explain the case of no nodule detected. XAI methods' performances are considered under a qualitative and quantitative comparison as feedback to improve the data quality and the model performance. Finally, we survey to assess doctors' and patients' trust in XAI explanations of the model's decisions on thyroid nodule images.
abstract_zh: 向最终用户解释深度学习模型的预测的能力是利用人工智能 (AI) 的力量进行医疗决策过程的一个重要特征，而医疗决策过程通常被认为是不透明且难以理解的。在本文中，我们应用最先进的可解释人工智能（XAI）方法来解释黑盒人工智能模型在甲状腺结节诊断应用中的预测。我们提出了新的基于统计的 XAI 方法，即核密度估计和密度图，来解释未检测到结节的情况。 XAI 方法的性能在定性和定量比较下被视为反馈，以提高数据质量和模型性能。最后，我们进行了调查，以评估医生和患者对 XAI 对甲状腺结节图像模型决策的解释的信任度。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过应用可解释人工智能（XAI）方法，提高甲状腺结节诊断中深度学习模型的可解释性，以增强医生和患者对其决策的信任。
AI.motivation: 医疗AI模型通常被视为黑盒，缺乏透明度，阻碍了其在临床中的信任与应用，因此需要有效的解释方法来提升医生和患者对AI诊断结果的信任。
AI.method: 提出基于核密度估计和密度图的统计XAI方法，用于解释无结节检测案例，并通过定性和定量分析评估其性能。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，所提方法有效提升了模型可解释性，有助于优化数据质量及模型性能，同时调查结果显示医生和患者对XAI解释的信任度有所提高。
AI.conclusion: 研究表明，可解释AI方法在提升医疗诊断模型透明度和用户信任方面具有重要作用，所提出的统计方法为临床应用提供了可行的解决方案。

authors_text: Johanna Bethge,Maik Pfefferkorn,Alexander Rose,Jan Peters,Rolf Findeisen
title: Model Predictive Control with Gaussian-Process-Supported Dynamical Constraints for Autonomous Vehicles
title_zh: 具有高斯过程支持的自动车辆动态约束的模型预测控制
abstract: We propose a model predictive control approach for autonomous vehicles that exploits learned Gaussian processes for predicting human driving behavior. The proposed approach employs the uncertainty about the GP's prediction to achieve safety. A multi-mode predictive control approach considers the possible intentions of the human drivers. While the intentions are represented by different Gaussian processes, their probabilities foreseen in the observed behaviors are determined by a suitable online classification. Intentions below a certain probability threshold are neglected to improve performance. The proposed multi-mode model predictive control approach with Gaussian process regression support enables repeated feasibility and probabilistic constraint satisfaction with high probability. The approach is underlined in simulation, considering real-world measurements for training the Gaussian processes.
summary: We propose a model predictive control approach for autonomous vehicles that exploits learned Gaussian processes for predicting human driving behavior. The proposed approach employs the uncertainty about the GP's prediction to achieve safety. A multi-mode predictive control approach considers the possible intentions of the human drivers. While the intentions are represented by different Gaussian processes, their probabilities foreseen in the observed behaviors are determined by a suitable online classification. Intentions below a certain probability threshold are neglected to improve performance. The proposed multi-mode model predictive control approach with Gaussian process regression support enables repeated feasibility and probabilistic constraint satisfaction with high probability. The approach is underlined in simulation, considering real-world measurements for training the Gaussian processes.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种用于自动驾驶车辆的模型预测控制方法，该方法利用学习的高斯过程来预测人类驾驶行为。所提出的方法利用全科医生预测的不确定性来实现安全。多模式预测控制方法考虑了人类驾驶员的可能意图。虽然意图由不同的高斯过程表示，但在观察到的行为中预见到的概率是由适当的在线分类确定的。为了提高性能，低于某个概率阈值的意图将被忽略。所提出的具有高斯过程回归支持的多模式模型预测控制方法能够以高概率实现重复可行性和概率约束满足。该方法在模拟中得到强调，考虑到训练高斯过程的真实测量。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于高斯过程支持的动力学约束模型预测控制方法，用于自动驾驶车辆，以预测人类驾驶行为并确保安全。
AI.motivation: 自动驾驶车辆需要准确预测人类驾驶行为并确保安全，传统方法难以处理不确定性与多意图场景。
AI.method: 利用高斯过程学习人类驾驶行为，通过不确定性量化实现安全控制；采用多模式预测控制，结合在线分类确定驾驶员意图概率并过滤低概率意图。
AI.result: 仿真结果验证了该方法在实际测量数据训练下的可行性与高概率约束满足能力，提升了自动驾驶性能。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过高斯过程与多模式控制结合，有效处理不确定性与多意图场景，为自动驾驶提供了更安全可靠的控制框架。

search_text: Model Predictive Control with Gaussian-Process-Supported Dynamical Constraints for Autonomous Vehicles 具有高斯过程支持的自动车辆动态约束的模型预测控制 We propose a model predictive control approach for autonomous vehicles that exploits learned Gaussian processes for predicting human driving behavior. The proposed approach employs the uncertainty about the GP's prediction to achieve safety. A multi-mode predictive control approach considers the possible intentions of the human drivers. While the intentions are represented by different Gaussian processes, their probabilities foreseen in the observed behaviors are determined by a suitable online classification. Intentions below a certain probability threshold are neglected to improve performance. The proposed multi-mode model predictive control approach with Gaussian process regression support enables repeated feasibility and probabilistic constraint satisfaction with high probability. The approach is underlined in simulation, considering real-world measurements for training the Gaussian processes. 我们提出了一种用于自动驾驶车辆的模型预测控制方法，该方法利用学习的高斯过程来预测人类驾驶行为。所提出的方法利用全科医生预测的不确定性来实现安全。多模式预测控制方法考虑了人类驾驶员的可能意图。虽然意图由不同的高斯过程表示，但在观察到的行为中预见到的概率是由适当的在线分类确定的。为了提高性能，低于某个概率阈值的意图将被忽略。所提出的具有高斯过程回归支持的多模式模型预测控制方法能够以高概率实现重复可行性和概率约束满足。该方法在模拟中得到强调，考虑到训练高斯过程的真实测量。 本文提出了一种基于高斯过程支持的动力学约束模型预测控制方法，用于自动驾驶车辆，以预测人类驾驶行为并确保安全。 Johanna Bethge,Maik Pfefferkorn,Alexander Rose,Jan Peters,Rolf Findeisen eess.SY,cs.LG,cs.RO,math.OC

authors_text: Roodabeh Safavi,Martin P. Seybold
title: B-Treaps Revised: Write Efficient Randomized Block Search Trees with High Load
title_zh: B-Treaps 修订版：编写高负载的高效随机块搜索树
abstract: Uniquely represented data structures represent each logical state with a unique storage state. We study the problem of maintaining a dynamic set of $n$ keys from a totally ordered universe in this context. We introduce a two-layer data structure called $(α,\varepsilon)$-Randomized Block Search Tree (RBST) that is uniquely represented and suitable for external memory. Though RBSTs naturally generalize the well-known binary Treaps, several new ideas are needed to analyze the {\em expected} search, update, and storage, efficiency in terms of block-reads, block-writes, and blocks stored. We prove that searches have $O(\varepsilon^{-1} + \log_αn)$ block-reads, that $(α, \varepsilon)$-RBSTs have an asymptotic load-factor of at least $(1-\varepsilon)$ for every $\varepsilon \in (0,1/2]$, and that dynamic updates perform $O(\varepsilon^{-1} + \log_α(n)/α)$ block-writes, i.e. $O(1/\varepsilon)$ writes if $α=Ω(\frac{\log n}{\log \log n} )$. Thus $(α, \varepsilon)$-RBSTs provide improved search, storage-, and write-efficiency bounds in regard to the known, uniquely represented B-Treap [Golovin; ICALP'09].
summary: Uniquely represented data structures represent each logical state with a unique storage state. We study the problem of maintaining a dynamic set of $n$ keys from a totally ordered universe in this context. We introduce a two-layer data structure called $(α,\varepsilon)$-Randomized Block Search Tree (RBST) that is uniquely represented and suitable for external memory. Though RBSTs naturally generalize the well-known binary Treaps, several new ideas are needed to analyze the {\em expected} search, update, and storage, efficiency in terms of block-reads, block-writes, and blocks stored. We prove that searches have $O(\varepsilon^{-1} + \log_αn)$ block-reads, that $(α, \varepsilon)$-RBSTs have an asymptotic load-factor of at least $(1-\varepsilon)$ for every $\varepsilon \in (0,1/2]$, and that dynamic updates perform $O(\varepsilon^{-1} + \log_α(n)/α)$ block-writes, i.e. $O(1/\varepsilon)$ writes if $α=Ω(\frac{\log n}{\log \log n} )$. Thus $(α, \varepsilon)$-RBSTs provide improved search, storage-, and write-efficiency bounds in regard to the known, uniquely represented B-Treap [Golovin; ICALP'09].
abstract_zh: 唯一表示的数据结构用唯一的存储状态表示每个逻辑状态。在这种情况下，我们研究了从全序宇宙中维护一组动态的 $n$ 键的问题。 We introduce a two-layer data structure called $(α,\varepsilon)$-Randomized Block Search Tree (RBST) that is uniquely represented and suitable for external memory. Though RBSTs naturally generalize the well-known binary Treaps, several new ideas are needed to analyze the {\em expected} search, update, and storage, efficiency in terms of block-reads, block-writes, and blocks stored. We prove that searches have $O(\varepsilon^{-1} + \log_αn)$ block-reads, that $(α, \varepsilon)$-RBSTs have an asymptotic load-factor of at least $(1-\varepsilon)$ for every $\varepsilon \in (0,1/2]$, and that dynamic updates perform $O(\varepsilon^{-1} + \log_α(n)/α)$ block-writes, i.e. $O(1/\varepsilon)$ writes if $α=Ω(\frac{\log n}{\log \log n} )$. Thus $(α, \varepsilon)$-RBSTs provide improved search, storage-, and write-efficiency bounds in regard to the known, uniquely represented B-Treap [Golovin; ICALP'09].

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为(α,ε)-随机块搜索树(RBST)的新型数据结构，旨在改进外部存储环境中动态键集的维护效率。
AI.motivation: 传统B-Treap在写操作效率和负载因子方面存在局限，需设计更高效的唯一表示数据结构以优化块读写性能。
AI.method: 通过引入双层结构(α,ε)-RBST，结合概率分析方法，对搜索、更新和存储效率进行块读写操作的理论分析。
AI.result: 搜索操作块读次数为O(ε⁻¹ + log_αn)，负载因子≥1-ε，更新操作块写次数为O(ε⁻¹ + log_αn/α)，当α足够大时写操作降至O(1/ε)。
AI.conclusion: (α,ε)-RBST在搜索、存储及写效率上优于现有B-Treap，为外部存储动态数据管理提供了更优解决方案。

authors_text: Malte J. Rasch,Fabio Carta,Omebayode Fagbohungbe,Tayfun Gokmen
title: Fast offset corrected in-memory training
title_zh: 快速偏移校正内存训练
abstract: In-memory computing with resistive crossbar arrays has been suggested to accelerate deep-learning workloads in highly efficient manner. To unleash the full potential of in-memory computing, it is desirable to accelerate the training as well as inference for large deep neural networks (DNNs). In the past, specialized in-memory training algorithms have been proposed that not only accelerate the forward and backward passes, but also establish tricks to update the weight in-memory and in parallel. However, the state-of-the-art algorithm (Tiki-Taka version 2 (TTv2)) still requires near perfect offset correction and suffers from potential biases that might occur due to programming and estimation inaccuracies, as well as longer-term instabilities of the device materials. Here we propose and describe two new and improved algorithms for in-memory computing (Chopped-TTv2 (c-TTv2) and Analog Gradient Accumulation with Dynamic reference (AGAD)), that retain the same runtime complexity but correct for any remaining offsets using choppers. These algorithms greatly relax the device requirements and thus expanding the scope of possible materials potentially employed for such fast in-memory DNN training.
summary: In-memory computing with resistive crossbar arrays has been suggested to accelerate deep-learning workloads in highly efficient manner. To unleash the full potential of in-memory computing, it is desirable to accelerate the training as well as inference for large deep neural networks (DNNs). In the past, specialized in-memory training algorithms have been proposed that not only accelerate the forward and backward passes, but also establish tricks to update the weight in-memory and in parallel. However, the state-of-the-art algorithm (Tiki-Taka version 2 (TTv2)) still requires near perfect offset correction and suffers from potential biases that might occur due to programming and estimation inaccuracies, as well as longer-term instabilities of the device materials. Here we propose and describe two new and improved algorithms for in-memory computing (Chopped-TTv2 (c-TTv2) and Analog Gradient Accumulation with Dynamic reference (AGAD)), that retain the same runtime complexity but correct for any remaining offsets using choppers. These algorithms greatly relax the device requirements and thus expanding the scope of possible materials potentially employed for such fast in-memory DNN training.
abstract_zh: 有人建议使用电阻交叉阵列的内存计算以高效的方式加速深度学习工作负载。为了充分发挥内存计算的潜力，需要加速大型深度神经网络 (DNN) 的训练和推理。过去，已经提出了专门的内存训练算法，不仅可以加速前向和反向传递，还可以建立在内存中并行更新权重的技巧。然而，最先进的算法（Tiki-Taka 版本 2 (TTv2)）仍然需要近乎完美的偏移校正，并且存在由于编程和估计不准确以及器件材料的长期不稳定性而可能出现的潜在偏差。在这里，我们提出并描述了两种新的和改进的内存计算算法（Chopped-TTv2 (c-TTv2) 和带有动态参考的模拟梯度累加 (AGAD)），它们保留相同的运行时复杂性，但使用斩波器纠正任何剩余偏移。这些算法大大放宽了设备要求，从而扩大了此类快速内存 DNN 训练可能使用的材料范围。

AI.tldr: 本文提出两种改进的内存中训练算法（c-TTv2和AGAD），通过使用choppers校正剩余偏移，提升内存计算中深度神经网络训练的效率和稳定性。
AI.motivation: 现有最先进的TTv2算法需要近乎完美的偏移校正，且受编程误差、估计不准确及器件长期不稳定性影响，限制了材料选择和应用范围。
AI.method: 设计两种新算法：Chopped-TTv2（c-TTv2）和Analog Gradient Accumulation with Dynamic reference（AGAD），利用choppers校正偏移，保持原有时间复杂度的同时降低器件精度要求。
AI.result: 新算法显著放宽器件要求，减少因偏移导致的偏差，提升训练稳定性，并扩展了可用于快速内存DNN训练的材料范围。
AI.conclusion: 通过改进偏移校正机制，本文算法为内存计算中的深度学习训练提供了更鲁棒的解决方案，降低了硬件约束并提升了材料兼容性。

search_text: Fast offset corrected in-memory training 快速偏移校正内存训练 In-memory computing with resistive crossbar arrays has been suggested to accelerate deep-learning workloads in highly efficient manner. To unleash the full potential of in-memory computing, it is desirable to accelerate the training as well as inference for large deep neural networks (DNNs). In the past, specialized in-memory training algorithms have been proposed that not only accelerate the forward and backward passes, but also establish tricks to update the weight in-memory and in parallel. However, the state-of-the-art algorithm (Tiki-Taka version 2 (TTv2)) still requires near perfect offset correction and suffers from potential biases that might occur due to programming and estimation inaccuracies, as well as longer-term instabilities of the device materials. Here we propose and describe two new and improved algorithms for in-memory computing (Chopped-TTv2 (c-TTv2) and Analog Gradient Accumulation with Dynamic reference (AGAD)), that retain the same runtime complexity but correct for any remaining offsets using choppers. These algorithms greatly relax the device requirements and thus expanding the scope of possible materials potentially employed for such fast in-memory DNN training. 有人建议使用电阻交叉阵列的内存计算以高效的方式加速深度学习工作负载。为了充分发挥内存计算的潜力，需要加速大型深度神经网络 (DNN) 的训练和推理。过去，已经提出了专门的内存训练算法，不仅可以加速前向和反向传递，还可以建立在内存中并行更新权重的技巧。然而，最先进的算法（Tiki-Taka 版本 2 (TTv2)）仍然需要近乎完美的偏移校正，并且存在由于编程和估计不准确以及器件材料的长期不稳定性而可能出现的潜在偏差。在这里，我们提出并描述了两种新的和改进的内存计算算法（Chopped-TTv2 (c-TTv2) 和带有动态参考的模拟梯度累加 (AGAD)），它们保留相同的运行时复杂性，但使用斩波器纠正任何剩余偏移。这些算法大大放宽了设备要求，从而扩大了此类快速内存 DNN 训练可能使用的材料范围。 本文提出两种改进的内存中训练算法（c-TTv2和AGAD），通过使用choppers校正剩余偏移，提升内存计算中深度神经网络训练的效率和稳定性。 Malte J. Rasch,Fabio Carta,Omebayode Fagbohungbe,Tayfun Gokmen cs.LG,cs.AR,cs.ET

authors_text: A. Bruno,C. Caudai,G. R. Leone,M. Martinelli,D. Moroni,F. Crotti
title: Medical Waste Sorting: a computer vision approach for assisted primary sorting
title_zh: 医疗废物分类：辅助初级分类的计算机视觉方法
comment: Accepted for inclusion in IWCIM 2023 @ ICASSP2023, June 5, 2023, Rhodes, Greece
comments: Accepted for inclusion in IWCIM 2023 @ ICASSP2023, June 5, 2023, Rhodes, Greece
abstract: Medical waste, i.e. waste produced during medical activities in hospitals, clinics and laboratories, represents hazardous waste whose management involves special care and high costs. However, this kind of waste contains a significant fraction of highly valued materials that can enter a circular economy process. To this end, in this paper, we propose a computer vision approach for assisting in the primary sorting of medical waste. The feasibility of our approach is demonstrated on a representative dataset we collected and made available to the community, with which we have trained a model that achieves 100\% accuracy, and a new dataset on which the trained model exhibits good generalization.
summary: Medical waste, i.e. waste produced during medical activities in hospitals, clinics and laboratories, represents hazardous waste whose management involves special care and high costs. However, this kind of waste contains a significant fraction of highly valued materials that can enter a circular economy process. To this end, in this paper, we propose a computer vision approach for assisting in the primary sorting of medical waste. The feasibility of our approach is demonstrated on a representative dataset we collected and made available to the community, with which we have trained a model that achieves 100\% accuracy, and a new dataset on which the trained model exhibits good generalization.
abstract_zh: 医疗废物，即医院、诊所和实验室医疗活动中产生的废物，是危险废物，其管理需要特殊照顾且成本高昂。然而，这种废物含有很大一部分可以进入循环经济过程的高价值材料。为此，在本文中，我们提出了一种计算机视觉方法来辅助医疗废物的初步分类。我们的方法的可行性在我们收集并提供给社区的代表性数据集上得到了证明，我们用它训练了一个达到 100% 准确率的模型，并且在一个新数据集上训练的模型表现出了良好的泛化性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于计算机视觉的医疗废物辅助初级分类方法。
AI.motivation: 医疗废物管理成本高且需特殊处理，但其中含有可循环利用的高价值材料，亟需高效分类技术以降低环境风险并提升资源利用率。
AI.method: 构建医疗废物数据集并训练计算机视觉模型，通过图像识别实现废物分类，验证模型在自有数据集和新数据集上的泛化能力。
AI.result: 在自有数据集上模型达到100%准确率，在新数据集上表现出良好的泛化性能。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为医疗废物智能分类提供了可行方案，公开的数据集可推动相关研究发展。

authors_text: Nick Willemstein,Saivimal Sridar,Herman van der Kooij,Ali Sadeghi
title: Soft insoles for estimating 3D ground reaction forces using 3D printed foam-like sensors
title_zh: 使用 3D 打印泡沫状传感器估算 3D 地面反作用力的软鞋垫
comment: Improved story, addition of more participants, results expanded but conclusions unchanged
comments: Improved story, addition of more participants, results expanded but conclusions unchanged
abstract: Sensorized insoles provide a tool for gait studies and health monitoring during daily life. For users to accept such insoles they need to be comfortable and lightweight. Previous work has already demonstrated that estimation of ground reaction forces (GRFs) is possible with insoles. However, these are often assemblies of commercial components restricting design freedom and customization. Within this work, we investigate using four 3D-printed soft foam-like sensors to sensorize an insole. These sensors were combined with system identification of Hammerstein-Wiener models to estimate the 3D GRFs, which were compared to values from an instrumented treadmill as the golden standard. It was observed that the four sensors behaved in line with the expected change in pressure distribution during the gait cycle. In addition, the identified (personalized) Hammerstein-Wiener models showed the best estimation performance (on average RMS error 9.3%, R^2=0.85 and mean absolute error (MAE) 7%) of the vertical, mediolateral, and anteroposterior GRFs. Thereby showing that these sensors can estimate the resulting 3D force reasonably well. These results for nine participants were comparable to or outperformed other works that used commercial FSRs with machine learning. The identified models did decrease in estimation performance over time but stayed on average 11.35% RMS and 8.6% MAE after a week with the Hammerstein-Wiener model seeming consistent between days two and seven. These results show promise for using 3D-printed soft piezoresistive foam-like sensors with system identification to be a viable approach for applications that require softness, lightweight, and customization such as wearable (force) sensors.

Previous work has already demonstrated that estimation of ground reaction forces (GRFs) is possible with insoles. However, these are often assemblies of commercial components restricting design freedom and customization. Within this work, we investigate using four 3D-printed soft foam-like sensors to sensorize an insole. These sensors were combined with system identification of Hammerstein-Wiener models to estimate the 3D GRFs, which were compared to values from an instrumented treadmill as the golden standard. It was observed that the four sensors behaved in line with the expected change in pressure distribution during the gait cycle. In addition, the identified (personalized) Hammerstein-Wiener models showed the best estimation performance (on average RMS error 9.3%, R^2=0.85 and mean absolute error (MAE) 7%) of the vertical, mediolateral, and anteroposterior GRFs. Thereby showing that these sensors can estimate the resulting 3D force reasonably well. These results for nine participants were comparable to or outperformed other works that used commercial FSRs with machine learning. The identified models did decrease in estimation performance over time but stayed on average 11.35% RMS and 8.6% MAE after a week with the Hammerstein-Wiener model seeming consistent between days two and seven. These results show promise for using 3D-printed soft piezoresistive foam-like sensors with system identification to be a viable approach for applications that require softness, lightweight, and customization such as wearable (force) sensors.
abstract_zh: 传感鞋垫为日常生活中的步态研究和健康监测提供了工具。为了让用户接受这种鞋垫，它们需要舒适且轻便。之前的工作已经证明，可以通过鞋垫来估计地面反作用力（GRF）。然而，这些通常是商业组件的组装，限制了设计自由度和定制。在这项工作中，我们研究了使用四个 3D 打印的软泡沫状传感器来感应鞋垫。这些传感器与 Hammerstein-Wiener 模型的系统识别相结合来估计 3D GRF，并将其与仪表跑步机的值进行比较作为黄金标准。据观察，四个传感器的行为与步态周期期间压力分布的预期变化一致。此外，所识别的（个性化）Hammerstein-Wiener 模型显示了垂直、内侧和前后 GRF 的最佳估计性能（平均 RMS 误差为 9.3%，R^2=0.85，平均绝对误差 (MAE) 7%）。从而表明这些传感器可以相当好地估计产生的 3D 力。九名参与者的结果与使用商业 FSR 和机器学习的其他作品相当或优于其他作品。随着时间的推移，确定的模型的估计性能确实有所下降，但一周后平均仍保持 11.35% RMS 和 8.6% MAE，Hammerstein-Wiener 模型在第二天到第七天之间似乎保持一致。这些结果表明，使用具有系统识别功能的 3D 打印软压阻泡沫类传感器有望成为需要柔软、轻量和定制的应用（例如可穿戴（力）传感器）的可行方法。

AI.tldr: 该论文提出了一种使用3D打印软泡沫状传感器的软质鞋垫，用于估算三维地面反作用力（GRF）。
AI.motivation: 传统传感器鞋垫依赖商业组件，限制了设计自由度和定制化能力，需开发更舒适、轻便且可定制的解决方案。
AI.method: 采用四个3D打印软泡沫传感器结合Hammerstein-Wiener系统识别模型，通过与仪器化跑步机数据对比验证估算效果。
AI.result: 四传感器表现符合步态周期压力分布预期，垂直、内外侧及前后向GRF估算误差分别为9.3%（RMS）、7%（MAE），R²=0.85，且模型在一周内性能下降有限。
AI.conclusion: 3D打印软压阻泡沫传感器结合系统识别方法可有效估算三维GRF，适用于需柔软性、轻量化及定制化的可穿戴传感器场景。

These results for nine participants were comparable to or outperformed other works that used commercial FSRs with machine learning. The identified models did decrease in estimation performance over time but stayed on average 11.35% RMS and 8.6% MAE after a week with the Hammerstein-Wiener model seeming consistent between days two and seven. These results show promise for using 3D-printed soft piezoresistive foam-like sensors with system identification to be a viable approach for applications that require softness, lightweight, and customization such as wearable (force) sensors. 传感鞋垫为日常生活中的步态研究和健康监测提供了工具。为了让用户接受这种鞋垫，它们需要舒适且轻便。之前的工作已经证明，可以通过鞋垫来估计地面反作用力（GRF）。然而，这些通常是商业组件的组装，限制了设计自由度和定制。在这项工作中，我们研究了使用四个 3D 打印的软泡沫状传感器来感应鞋垫。这些传感器与 Hammerstein-Wiener 模型的系统识别相结合来估计 3D GRF，并将其与仪表跑步机的值进行比较作为黄金标准。据观察，四个传感器的行为与步态周期期间压力分布的预期变化一致。此外，所识别的（个性化）Hammerstein-Wiener 模型显示了垂直、内侧和前后 GRF 的最佳估计性能（平均 RMS 误差为 9.3%，R^2=0.85，平均绝对误差 (MAE) 7%）。从而表明这些传感器可以相当好地估计产生的 3D 力。九名参与者的结果与使用商业 FSR 和机器学习的其他作品相当或优于其他作品。随着时间的推移，确定的模型的估计性能确实有所下降，但一周后平均仍保持 11.35% RMS 和 8.6% MAE，Hammerstein-Wiener 模型在第二天到第七天之间似乎保持一致。这些结果表明，使用具有系统识别功能的 3D 打印软压阻泡沫类传感器有望成为需要柔软、轻量和定制的应用（例如可穿戴（力）传感器）的可行方法。 该论文提出了一种使用3D打印软泡沫状传感器的软质鞋垫，用于估算三维地面反作用力（GRF）。 Nick Willemstein,Saivimal Sridar,Herman van der Kooij,Ali Sadeghi cs.RO

authors_text: Philipp Ennen,Po-Chun Hsu,Chan-Jan Hsu,Chang-Le Liu,Yen-Chen Wu,Yin-Hsiang Liao,Chin-Tung Lin,Da-Shan Shiu,Wei-Yun Ma
title: Extending the Pre-Training of BLOOM for Improved Support of Traditional Chinese: Models, Methods and Results
title_zh: 扩展 BLOOM 的预训练以改进对繁体中文的支持：模型、方法和结果
abstract: In this paper we present the multilingual language model BLOOM-zh that features enhanced support for Traditional Chinese. BLOOM-zh has its origins in the open-source BLOOM models presented by BigScience in 2022. Starting from released models, we extended the pre-training of BLOOM by additional 7.4 billion tokens in Traditional Chinese and English covering a variety of domains such as news articles, books, encyclopedias, educational materials as well as spoken language. In order to show the properties of BLOOM-zh, both existing and newly created benchmark scenarios are used for evaluating the performance. BLOOM-zh outperforms its predecessor on most Traditional Chinese benchmarks while maintaining its English capability. We release all our models to the research community.
summary: In this paper we present the multilingual language model BLOOM-zh that features enhanced support for Traditional Chinese. BLOOM-zh has its origins in the open-source BLOOM models presented by BigScience in 2022. Starting from released models, we extended the pre-training of BLOOM by additional 7.4 billion tokens in Traditional Chinese and English covering a variety of domains such as news articles, books, encyclopedias, educational materials as well as spoken language. In order to show the properties of BLOOM-zh, both existing and newly created benchmark scenarios are used for evaluating the performance. BLOOM-zh outperforms its predecessor on most Traditional Chinese benchmarks while maintaining its English capability. We release all our models to the research community.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了多语言语言模型 BLOOM-zh，其增强了对繁体中文的支持。 BLOOM-zh 起源于 BigScience 在 2022 年推出的开源 BLOOM 模型。从已发布的模型开始，我们对 BLOOM 的预训练进行了额外的 74 亿个繁体中文和英文代币的扩展，涵盖新闻文章、书籍、百科全书、教育材料和口语等多个领域。为了展示 BLOOM-zh 的特性，使用现有的和新创建的基准场景来评估性能。 BLOOM-zh 在大多数繁体中文基准测试中均优于其前身，同时保持其英语能力。我们向研究界发布了所有模型。

AI.tldr: 本文提出多语言模型BLOOM-zh，通过增加74亿传统中文和英文预训练数据，提升传统中文支持能力。
AI.motivation: 现有模型在传统中文处理上存在不足，需通过扩展预训练数据提升其语言能力。
AI.method: 基于BLOOM模型，新增涵盖新闻、书籍、百科等领域的74亿传统中文和英文数据进行预训练。
AI.result: BLOOM-zh在多数传统中文基准测试中优于原版，同时保持英文能力，模型已开源。
AI.conclusion: 扩展预训练数据有效提升传统中文支持，模型为研究社区提供新工具。

authors_text: Sara Abdollahi,Simon Gottschalk,Elena Demidova
title: LaSER: Language-Specific Event Recommendation
title_zh: LaSER：特定语言的活动推荐
comment: ef:Journal of Web Semantics, Volume 75, January 2023
comments: ef:Journal of Web Semantics, Volume 75, January 2023
abstract: While societal events often impact people worldwide, a significant fraction of events has a local focus that primarily affects specific language communities. Examples include national elections, the development of the Coronavirus pandemic in different countries, and local film festivals such as the César Awards in France and the Moscow International Film Festival in Russia. However, existing entity recommendation approaches do not sufficiently address the language context of recommendation. This article introduces the novel task of language-specific event recommendation, which aims to recommend events relevant to the user query in the language-specific context. This task can support essential information retrieval activities, including web navigation and exploratory search, considering the language context of user information needs. We propose LaSER, a novel approach toward language-specific event recommendation. LaSER blends the language-specific latent representations (embeddings) of entities and events and spatio-temporal event features in a learning to rank model. This model is trained on publicly available Wikipedia Clickstream data. The results of our user study demonstrate that LaSER outperforms state-of-the-art recommendation baselines by up to 33 percentage points in MAP@5 concerning the language-specific relevance of recommended events.
summary: While societal events often impact people worldwide, a significant fraction of events has a local focus that primarily affects specific language communities. Examples include national elections, the development of the Coronavirus pandemic in different countries, and local film festivals such as the César Awards in France and the Moscow International Film Festival in Russia. However, existing entity recommendation approaches do not sufficiently address the language context of recommendation. This article introduces the novel task of language-specific event recommendation, which aims to recommend events relevant to the user query in the language-specific context. This task can support essential information retrieval activities, including web navigation and exploratory search, considering the language context of user information needs. We propose LaSER, a novel approach toward language-specific event recommendation. LaSER blends the language-specific latent representations (embeddings) of entities and events and spatio-temporal event features in a learning to rank model. This model is trained on publicly available Wikipedia Clickstream data. The results of our user study demonstrate that LaSER outperforms state-of-the-art recommendation baselines by up to 33 percentage points in MAP@5 concerning the language-specific relevance of recommended events.
abstract_zh: 虽然社会事件经常影响世界各地的人们，但很大一部分事件的焦点主要是影响特定语言社区。例子包括全国选举、不同国家冠状病毒大流行的发展以及法国凯撒奖和俄罗斯莫斯科国际电影节等当地电影节。然而，现有的实体推荐方法不足以解决推荐的语言上下文。本文介绍了特定于语言的事件推荐的新颖任务，其目的是在特定于语言的上下文中推荐与用户查询相关的事件。该任务可以支持基本的信息检索活动，包括网络导航和探索性搜索，并考虑用户信息需求的语言上下文。我们提出了 LaSER，一种针对特定语言的事件推荐的新颖方法。 LaSER 在学习排序模型中融合了实体和事件的语言特定潜在表示（嵌入）以及时空事件特征。该模型是根据公开的维基百科点击流数据进行训练的。我们的用户研究结果表明，在推荐事件的语言特定相关性方面，LaSER 在 MAP@5 中比最先进的推荐基线高出 33 个百分点。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种针对语言特定场景的事件推荐方法LaSER，旨在根据用户的语言背景推荐相关事件。
AI.motivation: 现有实体推荐方法未能充分考虑事件的语言背景，而许多事件对特定语言社区影响显著，需在推荐中融入语言上下文。
AI.method: LaSER融合语言特定实体与事件的潜在表示及时空特征，基于排序模型进行训练，利用维基百科点击流数据。
AI.result: 用户研究表明，LaSER在语言特定相关性指标MAP@5上比现有方法高出33个百分点。
AI.conclusion: 本文提出了语言特定事件推荐的新任务及有效方法LaSER，验证了语言上下文在事件推荐中的关键作用。

authors_text: Kai Wang,Jianyang Gu,Daquan Zhou,Zheng Zhu,Wei Jiang,Yang You
title: DiM: Distilling Dataset into Generative Model
title_zh: DiM：将数据集提炼成生成模型
abstract: Dataset distillation reduces the network training cost by synthesizing small and informative datasets from large-scale ones. Despite the success of the recent dataset distillation algorithms, three drawbacks still limit their wider application: i). the synthetic images perform poorly on large architectures; ii). they need to be re-optimized when the distillation ratio changes; iii). the limited diversity restricts the performance when the distillation ratio is large. In this paper, we propose a novel distillation scheme to \textbf{D}istill information of large train sets \textbf{i}nto generative \textbf{M}odels, named DiM. Specifically, DiM learns to use a generative model to store the information of the target dataset. During the distillation phase, we minimize the differences in logits predicted by a models pool between real and generated images. At the deployment stage, the generative model synthesizes various training samples from random noises on the fly. Due to the simple yet effective designs, the trained DiM can be directly applied to different distillation ratios and large architectures without extra cost. We validate the proposed DiM across 4 datasets and achieve state-of-the-art results on all of them. To the best of our knowledge, we are the first to achieve higher accuracy on complex architectures than simple ones, such as 75.1\% with ResNet-18 and 72.6\% with ConvNet-3 on ten images per class of CIFAR-10. Besides, DiM outperforms previous methods with 10\% $\sim$ 22\% when images per class are 1 and 10 on the SVHN dataset.
summary: Dataset distillation reduces the network training cost by synthesizing small and informative datasets from large-scale ones. Despite the success of the recent dataset distillation algorithms, three drawbacks still limit their wider application: i). the synthetic images perform poorly on large architectures; ii). they need to be re-optimized when the distillation ratio changes; iii). the limited diversity restricts the performance when the distillation ratio is large. In this paper, we propose a novel distillation scheme to \textbf{D}istill information of large train sets \textbf{i}nto generative \textbf{M}odels, named DiM. Specifically, DiM learns to use a generative model to store the information of the target dataset. During the distillation phase, we minimize the differences in logits predicted by a models pool between real and generated images. At the deployment stage, the generative model synthesizes various training samples from random noises on the fly. Due to the simple yet effective designs, the trained DiM can be directly applied to different distillation ratios and large architectures without extra cost. We validate the proposed DiM across 4 datasets and achieve state-of-the-art results on all of them. To the best of our knowledge, we are the first to achieve higher accuracy on complex architectures than simple ones, such as 75.1\% with ResNet-18 and 72.6\% with ConvNet-3 on ten images per class of CIFAR-10. Besides, DiM outperforms previous methods with 10\% $\sim$ 22\% when images per class are 1 and 10 on the SVHN dataset.
abstract_zh: 数据集蒸馏通过从大规模数据集中合成小型且信息丰富的数据集来降低网络训练成本。尽管最近的数据集蒸馏算法取得了成功，但三个缺点仍然限制了它们的更广泛应用：i）。合成图像在大型架构上表现不佳； ii).当蒸馏比发生变化时，需要重新优化；三）。当精馏比较大时，有限的多样性限制了性能。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的蒸馏方案，将大型训练集 \textbf{D} 的信息 \textbf{i}n 蒸馏为生成 \textbf{M} 模型，称为 DiM。具体来说，DiM 学习使用生成模型来存储目标数据集的信息。在蒸馏阶段，我们最小化模型池预测的真实图像和生成图像之间的逻辑差异。在部署阶段，生成模型从动态随机噪声中合成各种训练样本。由于设计简单而有效，训练后的 DiM 可以直接应用于不同的蒸馏比和大型架构，而无需额外成本。我们在 4 个数据集上验证了所提出的 DiM，并在所有数据集上取得了最先进的结果。据我们所知，我们是第一个在复杂架构上实现比简单架构更高的准确率的人，例如在 CIFAR-10 的每类 10 幅图像上，ResNet-18 的准确率达到 75.1%，ConvNet-3 的准确率达到 72.6%。此外，当 SVHN 数据集上每类图像为 1 和 10 时，DiM 的性能优于以前的方法，为 10\% $\sim$ 22\%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出DiM方法，通过将大规模数据集信息蒸馏到生成模型中，解决现有数据集蒸馏方法在大模型、不同蒸馏比例和多样性方面的不足。
AI.motivation: 现有数据集蒸馏算法存在合成图像在大架构性能差、需重新优化蒸馏比例、多样性不足三大缺陷，限制了其应用。
AI.method: DiM通过生成模型存储目标数据集信息，在蒸馏阶段最小化模型池预测的真伪图像logit差异，部署时实时从噪声生成样本，支持不同蒸馏比例和大架构直接应用。
AI.result: 在4个数据集上取得SOTA结果，CIFAR-10上ResNet-18准确率75.1%（10图/类），SVHN数据集相比前人方法提升10%-22%。
AI.conclusion: DiM通过生成模型实现灵活高效的数据蒸馏，在复杂架构和高蒸馏比例下均优于现有方法，为模型训练成本优化提供新思路。
