search_text: DiM: Distilling Dataset into Generative Model DiM：将数据集提炼成生成模型 Dataset distillation reduces the network training cost by synthesizing small and informative datasets from large-scale ones. Despite the success of the recent dataset distillation algorithms, three drawbacks still limit their wider application: i). the synthetic images perform poorly on large architectures; ii). they need to be re-optimized when the distillation ratio changes; iii). the limited diversity restricts the performance when the distillation ratio is large. In this paper, we propose a novel distillation scheme to \textbf{D}istill information of large train sets \textbf{i}nto generative \textbf{M}odels, named DiM. Specifically, DiM learns to use a generative model to store the information of the target dataset. During the distillation phase, we minimize the differences in logits predicted by a models pool between real and generated images. At the deployment stage, the generative model synthesizes various training samples from random noises on the fly. Due to the simple yet effective designs, the trained DiM can be directly applied to different distillation ratios and large architectures without extra cost. We validate the proposed DiM across 4 datasets and achieve state-of-the-art results on all of them. To the best of our knowledge, we are the first to achieve higher accuracy on complex architectures than simple ones, such as 75.1\% with ResNet-18 and 72.6\% with ConvNet-3 on ten images per class of CIFAR-10. Besides, DiM outperforms previous methods with 10\% $\sim$ 22\% when images per class are 1 and 10 on the SVHN dataset. 数据集蒸馏通过从大规模数据集中合成小型且信息丰富的数据集来降低网络训练成本。尽管最近的数据集蒸馏算法取得了成功，但三个缺点仍然限制了它们的更广泛应用：i）。合成图像在大型架构上表现不佳； ii).当蒸馏比发生变化时，需要重新优化；三）。当精馏比较大时，有限的多样性限制了性能。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的蒸馏方案，将大型训练集 \textbf{D} 的信息 \textbf{i}n 蒸馏为生成 \textbf{M} 模型，称为 DiM。具体来说，DiM 学习使用生成模型来存储目标数据集的信息。在蒸馏阶段，我们最小化模型池预测的真实图像和生成图像之间的逻辑差异。在部署阶段，生成模型从动态随机噪声中合成各种训练样本。由于设计简单而有效，训练后的 DiM 可以直接应用于不同的蒸馏比和大型架构，而无需额外成本。我们在 4 个数据集上验证了所提出的 DiM，并在所有数据集上取得了最先进的结果。据我们所知，我们是第一个在复杂架构上实现比简单架构更高的准确率的人，例如在 CIFAR-10 的每类 10 幅图像上，ResNet-18 的准确率达到 75.1%，ConvNet-3 的准确率达到 72.6%。此外，当 SVHN 数据集上每类图像为 1 和 10 时，DiM 的性能优于以前的方法，为 10\% $\sim$ 22\%。 本文提出DiM方法，通过将大规模数据集信息蒸馏到生成模型中，解决现有数据集蒸馏方法在大模型、不同蒸馏比例和多样性方面的不足。 Kai Wang,Jianyang Gu,Daquan Zhou,Zheng Zhu,Wei Jiang,Yang You cs.CV

authors_text: Johannes Pitz,Lennart Röstel,Leon Sievers,Berthold Bäuml
title: Dextrous Tactile In-Hand Manipulation Using a Modular Reinforcement Learning Architecture
title_zh: 使用模块化强化学习架构进行灵巧的手动触觉操作
abstract: Dextrous in-hand manipulation with a multi-fingered robotic hand is a challenging task, esp. when performed with the hand oriented upside down, demanding permanent force-closure, and when no external sensors are used. For the task of reorienting an object to a given goal orientation (vs. infinitely spinning it around an axis), the lack of external sensors is an additional fundamental challenge as the state of the object has to be estimated all the time, e.g., to detect when the goal is reached. In this paper, we show that the task of reorienting a cube to any of the 24 possible goal orientations in a $π$/2-raster using the torque-controlled DLR-Hand II is possible. The task is learned in simulation using a modular deep reinforcement learning architecture: the actual policy has only a small observation time window of 0.5s but gets the cube state as an explicit input which is estimated via a deep differentiable particle filter trained on data generated by running the policy. In simulation, we reach a success rate of 92% while applying significant domain randomization. Via zero-shot Sim2Real-transfer on the real robotic system, all 24 goal orientations can be reached with a high success rate.
summary: Dextrous in-hand manipulation with a multi-fingered robotic hand is a challenging task, esp. when performed with the hand oriented upside down, demanding permanent force-closure, and when no external sensors are used. For the task of reorienting an object to a given goal orientation (vs. infinitely spinning it around an axis), the lack of external sensors is an additional fundamental challenge as the state of the object has to be estimated all the time, e.g., to detect when the goal is reached. In this paper, we show that the task of reorienting a cube to any of the 24 possible goal orientations in a $π$/2-raster using the torque-controlled DLR-Hand II is possible. The task is learned in simulation using a modular deep reinforcement learning architecture: the actual policy has only a small observation time window of 0.5s but gets the cube state as an explicit input which is estimated via a deep differentiable particle filter trained on data generated by running the policy. In simulation, we reach a success rate of 92% while applying significant domain randomization. Via zero-shot Sim2Real-transfer on the real robotic system, all 24 goal orientations can be reached with a high success rate.
abstract_zh: 使用多指机器人手进行灵巧的手动操作是一项具有挑战性的任务，尤其是。当手朝下执行时，需要永久强制闭合，并且不使用外部传感器。对于将对象重新定向到给定目标方向（相对于绕轴无限旋转）的任务，缺乏外部传感器是一个额外的基本挑战，因为必须始终估计对象的状态，例如，检测何时达到目标。在本文中，我们证明了使用扭矩控制的 DLR-Hand II 将立方体重新定向到 $π$/2 栅格中 24 个可能的目标方向中的任何一个的任务是可能的。该任务是使用模块化深度强化学习架构在模拟中学习的：实际策略只有 0.5 秒的小观察时间窗口，但将立方体状态作为显式输入，通过对运行策略生成的数据进行训练的深度可微粒子滤波器进行估计。在模拟中，我们在应用显着的域随机化时达到了 92% 的成功率。通过真实机器人系统上的零样本 Sim2Real 传输，可以以很高的成功率实现所有 24 个目标方向。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种模块化深度强化学习架构，用于实现无外部传感器情况下机械手对立方体的精细触觉在手操作，成功完成24种目标朝向的重定向任务。
AI.motivation: 多指机械手在无外部传感器且倒置状态下实现永久力闭合的精细操作面临重大挑战，尤其需要实时估计物体状态以检测目标朝向是否达成。
AI.method: 采用模块化深度强化学习架构，策略网络仅使用0.5秒观测窗口并结合深度微分粒子滤波器估计立方体状态，通过大量领域随机化训练后实现Sim2Real零样本迁移。
AI.result: 仿真环境中达到92%成功率，实际机器人系统成功实现所有24种π/2栅格化目标朝向的高成功率操作。
AI.conclusion: 该方法验证了模块化强化学习结合状态估计器在复杂无传感器触觉操作任务中的有效性，为高难度在手操作提供了新解决方案。

authors_text: Lukas Rustler,Jiri Matas,Matej Hoffmann
title: Efficient Visuo-Haptic Object Shape Completion for Robot Manipulation
title_zh: 用于机器人操作的高效视觉触觉物体形状完成
comment: ef:2023 IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS)
comments: ef:2023 IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS)
abstract: For robot manipulation, a complete and accurate object shape is desirable. Here, we present a method that combines visual and haptic reconstruction in a closed-loop pipeline. From an initial viewpoint, the object shape is reconstructed using an implicit surface deep neural network. The location with highest uncertainty is selected for haptic exploration, the object is touched, the new information from touch and a new point cloud from the camera are added, object position is re-estimated and the cycle is repeated. We extend Rustler et al. (2022) by using a new theoretically grounded method to determine the points with highest uncertainty, and we increase the yield of every haptic exploration by adding not only the contact points to the point cloud but also incorporating the empty space established through the robot movement to the object. Additionally, the solution is compact in that the jaws of a closed two-finger gripper are directly used for exploration. The object position is re-estimated after every robot action and multiple objects can be present simultaneously on the table. We achieve a steady improvement with every touch using three different metrics and demonstrate the utility of the better shape reconstruction in grasping experiments on the real robot. On average, grasp success rate increases from 63.3% to 70.4% after a single exploratory touch and to 82.7% after five touches. The collected data and code are publicly available (https://osf.io/j6rkd/, https://github.com/ctu-vras/vishac)
summary: For robot manipulation, a complete and accurate object shape is desirable. Here, we present a method that combines visual and haptic reconstruction in a closed-loop pipeline. From an initial viewpoint, the object shape is reconstructed using an implicit surface deep neural network. The location with highest uncertainty is selected for haptic exploration, the object is touched, the new information from touch and a new point cloud from the camera are added, object position is re-estimated and the cycle is repeated. We extend Rustler et al. (2022) by using a new theoretically grounded method to determine the points with highest uncertainty, and we increase the yield of every haptic exploration by adding not only the contact points to the point cloud but also incorporating the empty space established through the robot movement to the object. Additionally, the solution is compact in that the jaws of a closed two-finger gripper are directly used for exploration. The object position is re-estimated after every robot action and multiple objects can be present simultaneously on the table. We achieve a steady improvement with every touch using three different metrics and demonstrate the utility of the better shape reconstruction in grasping experiments on the real robot. On average, grasp success rate increases from 63.3% to 70.4% after a single exploratory touch and to 82.7% after five touches. The collected data and code are publicly available (https://osf.io/j6rkd/, https://github.com/ctu-vras/vishac)
abstract_zh: 对于机器人操纵来说，需要完整且准确的物体形状。在这里，我们提出了一种在闭环管道中结合视觉和触觉重建的方法。从最初的角度来看，物体形状是使用隐式表面深度神经网络重建的。选择不确定性最高的位置进行触觉探索，触摸物体，添加来自触摸的新信息和来自相机的新点云，重新估计物体位置并重复该循环。我们扩展 Rustler 等人。 （2022）通过使用一种新的理论基础方法来确定不确定性最高的点，我们不仅将接触点添加到点云中，而且还将通过机器人运动建立的空白空间合并到物体中，从而提高了每次触觉探索的产量。此外，该解决方案非常紧凑，因为闭合的两指夹具的钳口直接用于探索。每次机器人动作后都会重新估计物体位置，并且多个物体可以同时出现在桌子上。我们使用三种不同的指标实现了每次触摸的稳定改进，并展示了更好的形状重建在真实机器人抓取实验中的效用。平均而言，一次探索性触摸后抓取成功率从 63.3% 增加到 70.4%，五次触摸后增加到 82.7%。收集的数据和代码是公开的（https://osf.io/j6rkd/、https://github.com/ctu-vras/vishac）

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合视觉与触觉的闭环管道方法，用于机器人操作中的物体形状补全。
AI.motivation: 机器人操作需要完整准确的物体形状信息，但传统方法在效率和精度上存在不足。
AI.method: 使用隐式表面神经网络进行初始形状重建，通过触觉探索高不确定区域，并结合机器人运动产生的空闲空间数据迭代优化形状模型。
AI.result: 单次触觉探索后抓取成功率从63.3%提升至70.4%，五次后提升至82.7%。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过紧凑的触觉探索策略有效提升物体形状重建质量，从而增强机器人抓取性能。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种结合视觉与触觉的闭环管道方法，用于机器人操作中的物体形状补全。
ai_content.motivation: 机器人操作需要完整准确的物体形状信息，但传统方法在效率和精度上存在不足。
ai_content.method: 使用隐式表面神经网络进行初始形状重建，通过触觉探索高不确定区域，并结合机器人运动产生的空闲空间数据迭代优化形状模型。
ai_content.result: 单次触觉探索后抓取成功率从63.3%提升至70.4%，五次后提升至82.7%。
ai_content.conclusion: 该方法通过紧凑的触觉探索策略有效提升物体形状重建质量，从而增强机器人抓取性能。

search_text: Efficient Visuo-Haptic Object Shape Completion for Robot Manipulation 用于机器人操作的高效视觉触觉物体形状完成 For robot manipulation, a complete and accurate object shape is desirable. Here, we present a method that combines visual and haptic reconstruction in a closed-loop pipeline. From an initial viewpoint, the object shape is reconstructed using an implicit surface deep neural network. The location with highest uncertainty is selected for haptic exploration, the object is touched, the new information from touch and a new point cloud from the camera are added, object position is re-estimated and the cycle is repeated. We extend Rustler et al. (2022) by using a new theoretically grounded method to determine the points with highest uncertainty, and we increase the yield of every haptic exploration by adding not only the contact points to the point cloud but also incorporating the empty space established through the robot movement to the object. Additionally, the solution is compact in that the jaws of a closed two-finger gripper are directly used for exploration. The object position is re-estimated after every robot action and multiple objects can be present simultaneously on the table. We achieve a steady improvement with every touch using three different metrics and demonstrate the utility of the better shape reconstruction in grasping experiments on the real robot. On average, grasp success rate increases from 63.3% to 70.4% after a single exploratory touch and to 82.7% after five touches. The collected data and code are publicly available (https://osf.io/j6rkd/, https://github.com/ctu-vras/vishac) 对于机器人操纵来说，需要完整且准确的物体形状。在这里，我们提出了一种在闭环管道中结合视觉和触觉重建的方法。从最初的角度来看，物体形状是使用隐式表面深度神经网络重建的。选择不确定性最高的位置进行触觉探索，触摸物体，添加来自触摸的新信息和来自相机的新点云，重新估计物体位置并重复该循环。我们扩展 Rustler 等人。 （2022）通过使用一种新的理论基础方法来确定不确定性最高的点，我们不仅将接触点添加到点云中，而且还将通过机器人运动建立的空白空间合并到物体中，从而提高了每次触觉探索的产量。此外，该解决方案非常紧凑，因为闭合的两指夹具的钳口直接用于探索。每次机器人动作后都会重新估计物体位置，并且多个物体可以同时出现在桌子上。我们使用三种不同的指标实现了每次触摸的稳定改进，并展示了更好的形状重建在真实机器人抓取实验中的效用。平均而言，一次探索性触摸后抓取成功率从 63.3% 增加到 70.4%，五次触摸后增加到 82.7%。收集的数据和代码是公开的（https://osf.io/j6rkd/、https://github.com/ctu-vras/vishac） 本文提出一种结合视觉与触觉的闭环管道方法，用于机器人操作中的物体形状补全。 Lukas Rustler,Jiri Matas,Matej Hoffmann cs.RO

authors_text: Ramin Nakhli,Allen Zhang,Hossein Farahani,Amirali Darbandsari,Elahe Shenasa,Sidney Thiessen,Katy Milne,Jessica McAlpine,Brad Nelson,C Blake Gilks,Ali Bashashati
title: VOLTA: an Environment-Aware Contrastive Cell Representation Learning for Histopathology
title_zh: VOLTA：用于组织病理学的环境感知对比细胞表示学习
abstract: In clinical practice, many diagnosis tasks rely on the identification of cells in histopathology images. While supervised machine learning techniques require labels, providing manual cell annotations is time-consuming due to the large number of cells. In this paper, we propose a self-supervised framework (VOLTA) for cell representation learning in histopathology images using a novel technique that accounts for the cell's mutual relationship with its environment for improved cell representations. We subjected our model to extensive experiments on the data collected from multiple institutions around the world comprising of over 700,000 cells, four cancer types, and cell types ranging from three to six categories for each dataset. The results show that our model outperforms the state-of-the-art models in cell representation learning. To showcase the potential power of our proposed framework, we applied VOLTA to ovarian and endometrial cancers with very small sample sizes (10-20 samples) and demonstrated that our cell representations can be utilized to identify the known histotypes of ovarian cancer and provide novel insights that link histopathology and molecular subtypes of endometrial cancer. Unlike supervised deep learning models that require large sample sizes for training, we provide a framework that can empower new discoveries without any annotation data in situations where sample sizes are limited.

While supervised machine learning techniques require labels, providing manual cell annotations is time-consuming due to the large number of cells. In this paper, we propose a self-supervised framework (VOLTA) for cell representation learning in histopathology images using a novel technique that accounts for the cell's mutual relationship with its environment for improved cell representations. We subjected our model to extensive experiments on the data collected from multiple institutions around the world comprising of over 700,000 cells, four cancer types, and cell types ranging from three to six categories for each dataset. The results show that our model outperforms the state-of-the-art models in cell representation learning. To showcase the potential power of our proposed framework, we applied VOLTA to ovarian and endometrial cancers with very small sample sizes (10-20 samples) and demonstrated that our cell representations can be utilized to identify the known histotypes of ovarian cancer and provide novel insights that link histopathology and molecular subtypes of endometrial cancer. Unlike supervised deep learning models that require large sample sizes for training, we provide a framework that can empower new discoveries without any annotation data in situations where sample sizes are limited.
abstract_zh: 在临床实践中，许多诊断任务依赖于组织病理学图像中细胞的识别。虽然监督机器学习技术需要标签，但由于细胞数量庞大，提供手动细胞注释非常耗时。 In this paper, we propose a self-supervised framework (VOLTA) for cell representation learning in histopathology images using a novel technique that accounts for the cell's mutual relationship with its environment for improved cell representations. We subjected our model to extensive experiments on the data collected from multiple institutions around the world comprising of over 700,000 cells, four cancer types, and cell types ranging from three to six categories for each dataset.结果表明，我们的模型在细胞表示学习方面优于最先进的模型。 To showcase the potential power of our proposed framework, we applied VOLTA to ovarian and endometrial cancers with very small sample sizes (10-20 samples) and demonstrated that our cell representations can be utilized to identify the known histotypes of ovarian cancer and provide novel insights that link histopathology and molecular subtypes of endometrial cancer. Unlike supervised deep learning models that require large sample sizes for training, we provide a framework that can empower new discoveries without any annotation data in situations where sample sizes are limited.

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种自监督框架VOLTA，通过考虑细胞与环境的相互关系，实现组织病理学图像中无需标注的细胞表征学习。
AI.motivation: 传统监督学习依赖大量人工标注细胞数据，而病理图像中细胞数量庞大导致标注成本极高，且小样本场景下模型性能受限。
AI.method: 设计环境感知的对比学习框架，通过细胞与其局部环境的相互关系构建自监督任务，利用多机构大规模数据（70万+细胞）进行训练。
AI.result: 在4种癌症类型、3-6类细胞数据集上优于现有模型；在10-20小样本的卵巢癌和子宫内膜癌中，成功识别组织亚型并揭示分子分型关联。
AI.conclusion: VOLTA框架突破了传统方法对标注数据和样本量的依赖，在病理细胞表征学习中实现了小样本下的有效建模与新发现。

authors_text: Sungho Shin,Yeonguk Yu,Kyoobin Lee
title: Enhancing Low-resolution Face Recognition with Feature Similarity Knowledge Distillation
title_zh: 通过特征相似性知识蒸馏增强低分辨率人脸识别
abstract: In this study, we introduce a feature knowledge distillation framework to improve low-resolution (LR) face recognition performance using knowledge obtained from high-resolution (HR) images. The proposed framework transfers informative features from an HR-trained network to an LR-trained network by reducing the distance between them. A cosine similarity measure was employed as a distance metric to effectively align the HR and LR features. This approach differs from conventional knowledge distillation frameworks, which use the L_p distance metrics and offer the advantage of converging well when reducing the distance between features of different resolutions. Our framework achieved a 3% improvement over the previous state-of-the-art method on the AgeDB-30 benchmark without bells and whistles, while maintaining a strong performance on HR images. The effectiveness of cosine similarity as a distance metric was validated through statistical analysis, making our approach a promising solution for real-world applications in which LR images are frequently encountered. The code and pretrained models are publicly available on https://github.com/gist-ailab/feature-similarity-KD.
summary: In this study, we introduce a feature knowledge distillation framework to improve low-resolution (LR) face recognition performance using knowledge obtained from high-resolution (HR) images. The proposed framework transfers informative features from an HR-trained network to an LR-trained network by reducing the distance between them. A cosine similarity measure was employed as a distance metric to effectively align the HR and LR features. This approach differs from conventional knowledge distillation frameworks, which use the L_p distance metrics and offer the advantage of converging well when reducing the distance between features of different resolutions. Our framework achieved a 3% improvement over the previous state-of-the-art method on the AgeDB-30 benchmark without bells and whistles, while maintaining a strong performance on HR images. The effectiveness of cosine similarity as a distance metric was validated through statistical analysis, making our approach a promising solution for real-world applications in which LR images are frequently encountered. The code and pretrained models are publicly available on https://github.com/gist-ailab/feature-similarity-KD.
abstract_zh: 在本研究中，我们引入了一种特征知识蒸馏框架，利用从高分辨率（HR）图像中获得的知识来提高低分辨率（LR）人脸识别性能。 The proposed framework transfers informative features from an HR-trained network to an LR-trained network by reducing the distance between them. A cosine similarity measure was employed as a distance metric to effectively align the HR and LR features.这种方法与传统的知识蒸馏框架不同，传统的知识蒸馏框架使用 L_p 距离度量，并在减少不同分辨率的特征之间的距离时提供良好收敛的优势。我们的框架在 AgeDB-30 基准测试上比之前最先进的方法提高了 3%，同时在 HR 图像上保持了强劲的性能。通过统计分析验证了余弦相似度作为距离度量的有效性，这使得我们的方法成为经常遇到 LR 图像的实际应用的有前途的解决方案。 The code and pretrained models are publicly available on https://github.com/gist-ailab/feature-similarity-KD.

AI.tldr: 提出一种基于特征相似性的知识蒸馏框架，以提升低分辨率人脸识别性能。
AI.motivation: 传统知识蒸馏方法使用L_p距离在跨分辨率特征对齐中收敛效果不佳，需探索更有效的相似性度量。
AI.method: 通过余弦相似度度量，将高分辨率网络的特征知识迁移至低分辨率网络，缩小特征间距离。
AI.result: 在AgeDB-30基准上提升3%，且在高分辨率图像上保持强性能。
AI.conclusion: 余弦相似度作为度量有效，为实际低分辨率场景提供有效解决方案。

A cosine similarity measure was employed as a distance metric to effectively align the HR and LR features.这种方法与传统的知识蒸馏框架不同，传统的知识蒸馏框架使用 L_p 距离度量，并在减少不同分辨率的特征之间的距离时提供良好收敛的优势。我们的框架在 AgeDB-30 基准测试上比之前最先进的方法提高了 3%，同时在 HR 图像上保持了强劲的性能。通过统计分析验证了余弦相似度作为距离度量的有效性，这使得我们的方法成为经常遇到 LR 图像的实际应用的有前途的解决方案。 The code and pretrained models are publicly available on https://github.com/gist-ailab/feature-similarity-KD. 提出一种基于特征相似性的知识蒸馏框架，以提升低分辨率人脸识别性能。 Sungho Shin,Yeonguk Yu,Kyoobin Lee cs.CV

authors_text: Chi Wang,Susan Xueqing Liu,Ahmed H. Awadallah
title: Cost-Effective Hyperparameter Optimization for Large Language Model Generation Inference
title_zh: 用于大型语言模型生成推理的经济高效的超参数优化
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have sparked significant interest in their generative capabilities, leading to the development of various commercial applications. The high cost of using the models drives application builders to maximize the value of generation under a limited inference budget. This paper presents a study of optimizing inference hyperparameters such as the number of responses, temperature and max tokens, which significantly affects the utility/cost of text generation. We design a framework named EcoOptiGen which leverages economical hyperparameter optimization and cost-based pruning. Experiments with the GPT-3.5/GPT-4 models on a variety of tasks verify its effectiveness. EcoOptiGen is implemented in the `autogen' package of the FLAML library: \url{https://aka.ms/autogen}.
summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have sparked significant interest in their generative capabilities, leading to the development of various commercial applications. The high cost of using the models drives application builders to maximize the value of generation under a limited inference budget. This paper presents a study of optimizing inference hyperparameters such as the number of responses, temperature and max tokens, which significantly affects the utility/cost of text generation. We design a framework named EcoOptiGen which leverages economical hyperparameter optimization and cost-based pruning. Experiments with the GPT-3.5/GPT-4 models on a variety of tasks verify its effectiveness. EcoOptiGen is implemented in the `autogen' package of the FLAML library: \url{https://aka.ms/autogen}.
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型 (LLM) 引发了对其生成能力的极大兴趣，从而促进了各种商业应用程序的开发。使用模型的高昂成本促使应用程序构建者在有限的推理预算下最大化生成的价值。本文提出了一项优化推理超参数的研究，例如响应数量、温度和最大标记，这些参数显着影响文本生成的效用/成本。我们设计了一个名为 EcoOptiGen 的框架，它利用经济的超参数优化和基于成本的修剪。 GPT-3.5/GPT-4模型在各种任务上的实验验证了其有效性。 EcoOptiGen 在 FLAML 库的“autogen”包中实现：\url{https://aka.ms/autogen}。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为EcoOptiGen的框架，用于在有限的推理预算下优化大语言模型生成推理的超参数，以提高生成文本的效用与成本效益。
AI.motivation: 大语言模型的使用成本较高，应用开发者需在有限预算内最大化生成文本的价值，因此需要优化推理超参数以平衡效用与成本。
AI.method: 设计了EcoOptiGen框架，结合经济型超参数优化和基于成本的剪枝技术，通过调整响应数量、温度和最大令牌数等参数实现成本控制。
AI.result: 在GPT-3.5/GPT-4模型上进行的多任务实验验证了EcoOptiGen的有效性，证明其能显著提升生成任务的成本效益。
AI.conclusion: EcoOptiGen为大语言模型推理提供了高效的超参数优化方案，可在实际应用中实现生成质量与成本的平衡。

authors_text: Chenfei Wu,Shengming Yin,Weizhen Qi,Xiaodong Wang,Zecheng Tang,Nan Duan
title: Visual ChatGPT: Talking, Drawing and Editing with Visual Foundation Models
title_zh: Visual ChatGPT：使用 Visual Foundation 模型进行对话、绘图和编辑
abstract: ChatGPT is attracting a cross-field interest as it provides a language interface with remarkable conversational competency and reasoning capabilities across many domains. However, since ChatGPT is trained with languages, it is currently not capable of processing or generating images from the visual world. At the same time, Visual Foundation Models, such as Visual Transformers or Stable Diffusion, although showing great visual understanding and generation capabilities, they are only experts on specific tasks with one-round fixed inputs and outputs. To this end, We build a system called \textbf{Visual ChatGPT}, incorporating different Visual Foundation Models, to enable the user to interact with ChatGPT by 1) sending and receiving not only languages but also images 2) providing complex visual questions or visual editing instructions that require the collaboration of multiple AI models with multi-steps. 3) providing feedback and asking for corrected results. We design a series of prompts to inject the visual model information into ChatGPT, considering models of multiple inputs/outputs and models that require visual feedback. Experiments show that Visual ChatGPT opens the door to investigating the visual roles of ChatGPT with the help of Visual Foundation Models. Our system is publicly available at \url{https://github.com/microsoft/visual-chatgpt}.
summary: ChatGPT is attracting a cross-field interest as it provides a language interface with remarkable conversational competency and reasoning capabilities across many domains. However, since ChatGPT is trained with languages, it is currently not capable of processing or generating images from the visual world. At the same time, Visual Foundation Models, such as Visual Transformers or Stable Diffusion, although showing great visual understanding and generation capabilities, they are only experts on specific tasks with one-round fixed inputs and outputs. To this end, We build a system called \textbf{Visual ChatGPT}, incorporating different Visual Foundation Models, to enable the user to interact with ChatGPT by 1) sending and receiving not only languages but also images 2) providing complex visual questions or visual editing instructions that require the collaboration of multiple AI models with multi-steps. 3) providing feedback and asking for corrected results. We design a series of prompts to inject the visual model information into ChatGPT, considering models of multiple inputs/outputs and models that require visual feedback. Experiments show that Visual ChatGPT opens the door to investigating the visual roles of ChatGPT with the help of Visual Foundation Models. Our system is publicly available at \url{https://github.com/microsoft/visual-chatgpt}.
abstract_zh: ChatGPT 吸引了跨领域的兴趣，因为它提供了一个具有跨多个领域的卓越会话能力和推理能力的语言界面。然而，由于 ChatGPT 是用语言训练的，因此它目前无法处理或生成来自视觉世界的图像。与此同时，视觉基础模型，例如视觉变压器或稳定扩散，虽然表现出出色的视觉理解和生成能力，但它们只是具有一轮固定输入和输出的特定任务的专家。为此，我们构建了一个名为 \textbf{Visual ChatGPT} 的系统，包含不同的 Visual Foundation 模型，使用户能够通过以下方式与 ChatGPT 进行交互：1）不仅发送和接收语言，还发送和接收图像 2）提供复杂的视觉问题或可视化编辑指令，需要多个 AI 模型多步骤协作。 3）提供反馈并要求纠正结果。考虑到多个输入/输出的模型和需要视觉反馈的模型，我们设计了一系列提示将视觉模型信息注入ChatGPT。实验表明，Visual ChatGPT 为在 Visual Foundation 模型的帮助下研究 ChatGPT 的视觉角色打开了大门。我们的系统可在 \url{https://github.com/microsoft/visual-chatgpt} 上公开获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Visual ChatGPT系统，通过整合视觉基础模型（如Visual Transformer、Stable Diffusion），使ChatGPT具备图像交互、多步骤视觉任务处理及反馈修正能力。
AI.motivation: ChatGPT虽具备强大语言交互能力，但无法处理图像；而现有视觉模型仅擅长单一任务且缺乏多轮交互能力，难以满足复杂视觉需求。
AI.method: 构建多模态交互框架，集成多种视觉模型，设计支持多输入/输出及视觉反馈的提示词（prompt），实现语言与图像的联合处理及多步骤任务协作。
AI.result: 实验表明系统可有效处理复杂视觉问答与编辑任务，支持用户反馈修正结果，开源代码已发布（GitHub链接）。
AI.conclusion: Visual ChatGPT通过结合语言模型与视觉模型的优势，为探索AI在视觉领域的应用提供了新方向。

authors_text: Sankeerth Durvasula,Yushi Guan,Nandita Vijaykumar
title: EvConv: Fast CNN Inference on Event Camera Inputs For High-Speed Robot Perception
title_zh: EvConv：对事件摄像机输入进行快速 CNN 推理，实现高速机器人感知
abstract: Event cameras capture visual information with a high temporal resolution and a wide dynamic range. This enables capturing visual information at fine time granularities (e.g., microseconds) in rapidly changing environments. This makes event cameras highly useful for high-speed robotics tasks involving rapid motion, such as high-speed perception, object tracking, and control. However, convolutional neural network inference on event camera streams cannot currently perform real-time inference at the high speeds at which event cameras operate - current CNN inference times are typically closer in order of magnitude to the frame rates of regular frame-based cameras. Real-time inference at event camera rates is necessary to fully leverage the high frequency and high temporal resolution that event cameras offer. This paper presents EvConv, a new approach to enable fast inference on CNNs for inputs from event cameras. We observe that consecutive inputs to the CNN from an event camera have only small differences between them. Thus, we propose to perform inference on the difference between consecutive input tensors, or the increment. This enables a significant reduction in the number of floating-point operations required (and thus the inference latency) because increments are very sparse. We design EvConv to leverage the irregular sparsity in increments from event cameras and to retain the sparsity of these increments across all layers of the network. We demonstrate a reduction in the number of floating operations required in the forward pass by up to 98%. We also demonstrate a speedup of up to 1.6X for inference using CNNs for tasks such as depth estimation, object recognition, and optical flow estimation, with almost no loss in accuracy.
summary: Event cameras capture visual information with a high temporal resolution and a wide dynamic range. This enables capturing visual information at fine time granularities (e.g., microseconds) in rapidly changing environments. This makes event cameras highly useful for high-speed robotics tasks involving rapid motion, such as high-speed perception, object tracking, and control. However, convolutional neural network inference on event camera streams cannot currently perform real-time inference at the high speeds at which event cameras operate - current CNN inference times are typically closer in order of magnitude to the frame rates of regular frame-based cameras. Real-time inference at event camera rates is necessary to fully leverage the high frequency and high temporal resolution that event cameras offer. This paper presents EvConv, a new approach to enable fast inference on CNNs for inputs from event cameras. We observe that consecutive inputs to the CNN from an event camera have only small differences between them. Thus, we propose to perform inference on the difference between consecutive input tensors, or the increment. This enables a significant reduction in the number of floating-point operations required (and thus the inference latency) because increments are very sparse. We design EvConv to leverage the irregular sparsity in increments from event cameras and to retain the sparsity of these increments across all layers of the network. We demonstrate a reduction in the number of floating operations required in the forward pass by up to 98%. We also demonstrate a speedup of up to 1.6X for inference using CNNs for tasks such as depth estimation, object recognition, and optical flow estimation, with almost no loss in accuracy.
abstract_zh: Event cameras capture visual information with a high temporal resolution and a wide dynamic range. This enables capturing visual information at fine time granularities (e.g., microseconds) in rapidly changing environments.这使得事件相机对于涉及快速运动的高速机器人任务非常有用，例如高速感知、对象跟踪和控制。然而，事件摄像机流上的卷积神经网络推理目前无法以事件摄像机运行的高速执行实时推理 - 当前的 CNN 推理时间通常在数量级上更接近基于常规帧的摄像机的帧速率。为了充分利用事件摄像机提供的高频和高时间分辨率，必须以事件摄像机速率进行实时推理。 This paper presents EvConv, a new approach to enable fast inference on CNNs for inputs from event cameras. We observe that consecutive inputs to the CNN from an event camera have only small differences between them. Thus, we propose to perform inference on the difference between consecutive input tensors, or the increment.由于增量非常稀疏，因此可以显着减少所需的浮点运算数量（从而减少推理延迟）。我们设计 EvConv 是为了利用事件摄像机增量中的不规则稀疏性，并在网络的所有层上保留这些增量的稀疏性。 We demonstrate a reduction in the number of floating operations required in the forward pass by up to 98%.我们还展示了使用 CNN 进行深度估计、对象识别和光流估计等任务的推理速度最高可达 1.6 倍，而且精度几乎没有损失。

search_text: EvConv: Fast CNN Inference on Event Camera Inputs For High-Speed Robot Perception EvConv：对事件摄像机输入进行快速 CNN 推理，实现高速机器人感知 Event cameras capture visual information with a high temporal resolution and a wide dynamic range. This enables capturing visual information at fine time granularities (e.g., microseconds) in rapidly changing environments. This makes event cameras highly useful for high-speed robotics tasks involving rapid motion, such as high-speed perception, object tracking, and control. However, convolutional neural network inference on event camera streams cannot currently perform real-time inference at the high speeds at which event cameras operate - current CNN inference times are typically closer in order of magnitude to the frame rates of regular frame-based cameras. Real-time inference at event camera rates is necessary to fully leverage the high frequency and high temporal resolution that event cameras offer. This paper presents EvConv, a new approach to enable fast inference on CNNs for inputs from event cameras. We observe that consecutive inputs to the CNN from an event camera have only small differences between them. Thus, we propose to perform inference on the difference between consecutive input tensors, or the increment. This enables a significant reduction in the number of floating-point operations required (and thus the inference latency) because increments are very sparse. We design EvConv to leverage the irregular sparsity in increments from event cameras and to retain the sparsity of these increments across all layers of the network. We demonstrate a reduction in the number of floating operations required in the forward pass by up to 98%. We also demonstrate a speedup of up to 1.6X for inference using CNNs for tasks such as depth estimation, object recognition, and optical flow estimation, with almost no loss in accuracy. Event cameras capture visual information with a high temporal resolution and a wide dynamic range. This enables capturing visual information at fine time granularities (e.g., microseconds) in rapidly changing environments.这使得事件相机对于涉及快速运动的高速机器人任务非常有用，例如高速感知、对象跟踪和控制。然而，事件摄像机流上的卷积神经网络推理目前无法以事件摄像机运行的高速执行实时推理 - 当前的 CNN 推理时间通常在数量级上更接近基于常规帧的摄像机的帧速率。为了充分利用事件摄像机提供的高频和高时间分辨率，必须以事件摄像机速率进行实时推理。 This paper presents EvConv, a new approach to enable fast inference on CNNs for inputs from event cameras. We observe that consecutive inputs to the CNN from an event camera have only small differences between them. Thus, we propose to perform inference on the difference between consecutive input tensors, or the increment.由于增量非常稀疏，因此可以显着减少所需的浮点运算数量（从而减少推理延迟）。我们设计 EvConv 是为了利用事件摄像机增量中的不规则稀疏性，并在网络的所有层上保留这些增量的稀疏性。 We demonstrate a reduction in the number of floating operations required in the forward pass by up to 98%.我们还展示了使用 CNN 进行深度估计、对象识别和光流估计等任务的推理速度最高可达 1.6 倍，而且精度几乎没有损失。 本文提出EvConv方法，通过利用事件相机输入的稀疏性差异，实现高速机器人感知中的快速CNN推理。 Sankeerth Durvasula,Yushi Guan,Nandita Vijaykumar cs.RO,cs.CV

authors_text: Rui Hu,Jianan Cui,Chengjin Yu,Yunmei Chen,Huafeng Liu
title: STPDnet: Spatial-temporal convolutional primal dual network for dynamic PET image reconstruction
title_zh: STPDnet：用于动态 PET 图像重建的时空卷积原始对偶网络
abstract: Dynamic positron emission tomography (dPET) image reconstruction is extremely challenging due to the limited counts received in individual frame. In this paper, we propose a spatial-temporal convolutional primal dual network (STPDnet) for dynamic PET image reconstruction. Both spatial and temporal correlations are encoded by 3D convolution operators. The physical projection of PET is embedded in the iterative learning process of the network, which provides the physical constraints and enhances interpretability. The experiments of real rat scan data have shown that the proposed method can achieve substantial noise reduction in both temporal and spatial domains and outperform the maximum likelihood expectation maximization (MLEM), spatial-temporal kernel method (KEM-ST), DeepPET and Learned Primal Dual (LPD).
summary: Dynamic positron emission tomography (dPET) image reconstruction is extremely challenging due to the limited counts received in individual frame. In this paper, we propose a spatial-temporal convolutional primal dual network (STPDnet) for dynamic PET image reconstruction. Both spatial and temporal correlations are encoded by 3D convolution operators. The physical projection of PET is embedded in the iterative learning process of the network, which provides the physical constraints and enhances interpretability. The experiments of real rat scan data have shown that the proposed method can achieve substantial noise reduction in both temporal and spatial domains and outperform the maximum likelihood expectation maximization (MLEM), spatial-temporal kernel method (KEM-ST), DeepPET and Learned Primal Dual (LPD).
abstract_zh: 由于单个帧中接收到的计数有限，动态正电子发射断层扫描 (dPET) 图像重建极具挑战性。 In this paper, we propose a spatial-temporal convolutional primal dual network (STPDnet) for dynamic PET image reconstruction. Both spatial and temporal correlations are encoded by 3D convolution operators. PET的物理投影嵌入到网络的迭代学习过程中，提供了物理约束并增强了可解释性。真实大鼠扫描数据的实验表明，该方法可以在时域和空间域上实现大幅降噪，并且优于最大似然期望最大化（MLEM）、时空核方法（KEM-ST）、DeepPET和Learned Primal Dual（LPD）。

AI.tldr: 提出STPDnet网络以解决动态PET图像重建中的低计数挑战
AI.motivation: 动态PET因单帧计数有限导致重建困难，现有方法效果不佳
AI.method: 结合3D卷积编码时空相关性，嵌入PET物理投影到网络迭代学习中
AI.result: 在真实大鼠数据中，STPDnet在时空降噪上优于MLEM、KEM-ST、DeepPET和LPD
AI.conclusion: STPDnet通过物理约束和时空建模有效提升了动态PET图像重建质量

authors_text: Xin Yan,Zuchao Li,Lefei Zhang
title: Centroid-centered Modeling for Efficient Vision Transformer Pre-training
title_zh: 用于高效 Vision Transformer 预训练的质心建模
abstract: Masked Image Modeling (MIM) is a new self-supervised vision pre-training paradigm using a Vision Transformer (ViT). Previous works can be pixel-based or token-based, using original pixels or discrete visual tokens from parametric tokenizer models, respectively. Our proposed centroid-based approach, CCViT, leverages k-means clustering to obtain centroids for image modeling without supervised training of the tokenizer model, which only takes seconds to create. This non-parametric centroid tokenizer only takes seconds to create and is faster for token inference. The centroids can represent both patch pixels and index tokens with the property of local invariance. Specifically, we adopt patch masking and centroid replacing strategies to construct corrupted inputs, and two stacked encoder blocks to predict corrupted patch tokens and reconstruct original patch pixels. Experiments show that our CCViT achieves 84.4% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet-1K classification with ViT-B and 86.0% with ViT-L. We also transfer our pre-trained model to other downstream tasks. Our approach achieves competitive results with recent baselines without external supervision and distillation training from other models.
summary: Masked Image Modeling (MIM) is a new self-supervised vision pre-training paradigm using a Vision Transformer (ViT). Previous works can be pixel-based or token-based, using original pixels or discrete visual tokens from parametric tokenizer models, respectively. Our proposed centroid-based approach, CCViT, leverages k-means clustering to obtain centroids for image modeling without supervised training of the tokenizer model, which only takes seconds to create. This non-parametric centroid tokenizer only takes seconds to create and is faster for token inference. The centroids can represent both patch pixels and index tokens with the property of local invariance. Specifically, we adopt patch masking and centroid replacing strategies to construct corrupted inputs, and two stacked encoder blocks to predict corrupted patch tokens and reconstruct original patch pixels. Experiments show that our CCViT achieves 84.4% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet-1K classification with ViT-B and 86.0% with ViT-L. We also transfer our pre-trained model to other downstream tasks. Our approach achieves competitive results with recent baselines without external supervision and distillation training from other models.
abstract_zh: 掩模图像建模 (MIM) 是一种使用视觉变换器 (ViT) 的新型自监督视觉预训练范例。以前的作品可以是基于像素的或基于标记的，分别使用来自参数标记器模型的原始像素或离散视觉标记。我们提出的基于质心的方法 CCViT，利用 k 均值聚类来获取图像建模的质心，无需对分词器模型进行监督训练，只需几秒钟即可创建。这种非参数质心标记生成器只需几秒钟即可创建，并且标记推理速度更快。质心可以表示具有局部不变性的块像素和索引标记。具体来说，我们采用补丁掩码和质心替换策略来构造损坏的输入，并采用两个堆叠编码器块来预测损坏的补丁标记并重建原始补丁像素。实验表明，我们的 CCViT 在使用 ViT-B 的 ImageNet-1K 分类上实现了 84.4% 的 top-1 准确率，使用 ViT-L 实现了 86.0% 的 top-1 准确率。我们还将预训练模型转移到其他下游任务。我们的方法在没有外部监督和其他模型的蒸馏训练的情况下，在最近的基线上取得了有竞争力的结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于质心的视觉Transformer预训练方法CCViT，通过k-means聚类生成图像质心作为非参数化分词器，提升预训练效率。
AI.motivation: 传统视觉Transformer预训练依赖像素或参数化分词器，存在计算开销大或需监督训练的问题，需更高效的非参数化分词方案。
AI.method: 利用k-means聚类快速生成图像质心，结合块掩码和质心替换策略构建损坏输入，采用双编码器块预测损坏块并重建原始像素。
AI.result: 在ImageNet-1K分类任务中，ViT-B达到84.4%、ViT-L达到86.0%的Top-1准确率，在其他下游任务中也表现优异。
AI.conclusion: CCViT通过质心建模实现了无需外部监督和模型蒸馏的高效预训练，验证了非参数化分词器在视觉Transformer中的有效性。

Specifically, we adopt patch masking and centroid replacing strategies to construct corrupted inputs, and two stacked encoder blocks to predict corrupted patch tokens and reconstruct original patch pixels. Experiments show that our CCViT achieves 84.4% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet-1K classification with ViT-B and 86.0% with ViT-L. We also transfer our pre-trained model to other downstream tasks. Our approach achieves competitive results with recent baselines without external supervision and distillation training from other models. 掩模图像建模 (MIM) 是一种使用视觉变换器 (ViT) 的新型自监督视觉预训练范例。以前的作品可以是基于像素的或基于标记的，分别使用来自参数标记器模型的原始像素或离散视觉标记。我们提出的基于质心的方法 CCViT，利用 k 均值聚类来获取图像建模的质心，无需对分词器模型进行监督训练，只需几秒钟即可创建。这种非参数质心标记生成器只需几秒钟即可创建，并且标记推理速度更快。质心可以表示具有局部不变性的块像素和索引标记。具体来说，我们采用补丁掩码和质心替换策略来构造损坏的输入，并采用两个堆叠编码器块来预测损坏的补丁标记并重建原始补丁像素。实验表明，我们的 CCViT 在使用 ViT-B 的 ImageNet-1K 分类上实现了 84.4% 的 top-1 准确率，使用 ViT-L 实现了 86.0% 的 top-1 准确率。我们还将预训练模型转移到其他下游任务。我们的方法在没有外部监督和其他模型的蒸馏训练的情况下，在最近的基线上取得了有竞争力的结果。 本文提出一种基于质心的视觉Transformer预训练方法CCViT，通过k-means聚类生成图像质心作为非参数化分词器，提升预训练效率。 Xin Yan,Zuchao Li,Lefei Zhang cs.CV

authors_text: Rui Hu,Yunmei Chen,Kyungsang Kim,Marcio Aloisio Bezerra Cavalcanti Rockenbach,Quanzheng Li,Huafeng Liu
title: DULDA: Dual-domain Unsupervised Learned Descent Algorithm for PET image reconstruction
title_zh: DULDA：用于 PET 图像重建的双域无监督学习下降算法
abstract: Deep learning based PET image reconstruction methods have achieved promising results recently. However, most of these methods follow a supervised learning paradigm, which rely heavily on the availability of high-quality training labels. In particular, the long scanning time required and high radiation exposure associated with PET scans make obtaining this labels impractical. In this paper, we propose a dual-domain unsupervised PET image reconstruction method based on learned decent algorithm, which reconstructs high-quality PET images from sinograms without the need for image labels. Specifically, we unroll the proximal gradient method with a learnable l2,1 norm for PET image reconstruction problem. The training is unsupervised, using measurement domain loss based on deep image prior as well as image domain loss based on rotation equivariance property. The experimental results domonstrate the superior performance of proposed method compared with maximum likelihood expectation maximazation (MLEM), total-variation regularized EM (EM-TV) and deep image prior based method (DIP).
summary: Deep learning based PET image reconstruction methods have achieved promising results recently. However, most of these methods follow a supervised learning paradigm, which rely heavily on the availability of high-quality training labels. In particular, the long scanning time required and high radiation exposure associated with PET scans make obtaining this labels impractical. In this paper, we propose a dual-domain unsupervised PET image reconstruction method based on learned decent algorithm, which reconstructs high-quality PET images from sinograms without the need for image labels. Specifically, we unroll the proximal gradient method with a learnable l2,1 norm for PET image reconstruction problem. The training is unsupervised, using measurement domain loss based on deep image prior as well as image domain loss based on rotation equivariance property. The experimental results domonstrate the superior performance of proposed method compared with maximum likelihood expectation maximazation (MLEM), total-variation regularized EM (EM-TV) and deep image prior based method (DIP).
abstract_zh: 基于深度学习的PET图像重建方法最近取得了可喜的成果。然而，这些方法大多数都遵循监督学习范式，这在很大程度上依赖于高质量训练标签的可用性。特别是，PET 扫描所需的长扫描时间和高辐射暴露使得获得这种标签不切实际。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于学习体面算法的双域无监督 PET 图像重建方法，该方法可以从正弦图中重建高质量的 PET 图像，而无需图像标签。具体来说，我们针对 PET 图像重建问题展开了具有可学习 l2,1 范数的近端梯度方法。训练是无监督的，使用基于深度图像先验的测量域损失以及基于旋转等方差特性的图像域损失。实验结果表明，与最大似然期望最大化（MLEM）、全变分正则化EM（EM-TV）和基于深度图像先验的方法（DIP）相比，该方法具有优越的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无需图像标签的双域无监督学习算法DULDA，用于PET图像重建。
AI.motivation: 现有PET图像重建的深度学习方法依赖大量高质量标签，但PET扫描的高辐射和长耗时使标签获取不切实际。
AI.method: 基于可学习l2,1范数展开近端梯度法，结合测量域深度图像先验损失与图像域旋转等变性损失进行无监督训练。
AI.result: 实验表明DULDA在重建质量上优于MLEM、EM-TV和DIP等现有方法。
AI.conclusion: 双域无监督学习框架为PET图像重建提供了无需标签的可行方案，具有重要应用潜力。

authors_text: Lennert De Smet,Pedro Zuidberg Dos Martires,Robin Manhaeve,Giuseppe Marra,Angelika Kimmig,Luc De Raedt
title: Neural Probabilistic Logic Programming in Discrete-Continuous Domains
title_zh: 离散连续域中的神经概率逻辑编程
abstract: Neural-symbolic AI (NeSy) allows neural networks to exploit symbolic background knowledge in the form of logic. It has been shown to aid learning in the limited data regime and to facilitate inference on out-of-distribution data. Probabilistic NeSy focuses on integrating neural networks with both logic and probability theory, which additionally allows learning under uncertainty. A major limitation of current probabilistic NeSy systems, such as DeepProbLog, is their restriction to finite probability distributions, i.e., discrete random variables. In contrast, deep probabilistic programming (DPP) excels in modelling and optimising continuous probability distributions. Hence, we introduce DeepSeaProbLog, a neural probabilistic logic programming language that incorporates DPP techniques into NeSy. Doing so results in the support of inference and learning of both discrete and continuous probability distributions under logical constraints. Our main contributions are 1) the semantics of DeepSeaProbLog and its corresponding inference algorithm, 2) a proven asymptotically unbiased learning algorithm, and 3) a series of experiments that illustrate the versatility of our approach.
summary: Neural-symbolic AI (NeSy) allows neural networks to exploit symbolic background knowledge in the form of logic. It has been shown to aid learning in the limited data regime and to facilitate inference on out-of-distribution data. Probabilistic NeSy focuses on integrating neural networks with both logic and probability theory, which additionally allows learning under uncertainty. A major limitation of current probabilistic NeSy systems, such as DeepProbLog, is their restriction to finite probability distributions, i.e., discrete random variables. In contrast, deep probabilistic programming (DPP) excels in modelling and optimising continuous probability distributions. Hence, we introduce DeepSeaProbLog, a neural probabilistic logic programming language that incorporates DPP techniques into NeSy. Doing so results in the support of inference and learning of both discrete and continuous probability distributions under logical constraints. Our main contributions are 1) the semantics of DeepSeaProbLog and its corresponding inference algorithm, 2) a proven asymptotically unbiased learning algorithm, and 3) a series of experiments that illustrate the versatility of our approach.
abstract_zh: Neural-symbolic AI (NeSy) allows neural networks to exploit symbolic background knowledge in the form of logic. It has been shown to aid learning in the limited data regime and to facilitate inference on out-of-distribution data. Probabilistic NeSy focuses on integrating neural networks with both logic and probability theory, which additionally allows learning under uncertainty. A major limitation of current probabilistic NeSy systems, such as DeepProbLog, is their restriction to finite probability distributions, i.e., discrete random variables. In contrast, deep probabilistic programming (DPP) excels in modelling and optimising continuous probability distributions.因此，我们引入 DeepSeaProbLog，一种将 DPP 技术融入 NeSy 的神经概率逻辑编程语言。 Doing so results in the support of inference and learning of both discrete and continuous probability distributions under logical constraints. Our main contributions are 1) the semantics of DeepSeaProbLog and its corresponding inference algorithm, 2) a proven asymptotically unbiased learning algorithm, and 3) a series of experiments that illustrate the versatility of our approach.

AI.tldr: 本文提出DeepSeaProbLog，一种结合深度概率编程技术的神经概率逻辑编程语言，支持在离散-连续域中进行逻辑约束下的概率推理与学习。
AI.motivation: 现有概率神经符号系统（如DeepProbLog）仅支持离散概率分布，难以处理实际场景中常见的连续变量问题，而深度概率编程（DPP）在建模连续分布方面表现优异，需将其与神经符号系统结合。
AI.method: 设计DeepSeaProbLog的语义与推理算法，整合深度概率编程技术，提出渐进无偏的学习算法，并通过多组实验验证方法的有效性。
AI.result: 实验表明DeepSeaProbLog能够有效处理离散与连续概率分布的联合推理，展示了方法在复杂场景下的灵活性与优势。
AI.conclusion: DeepSeaProbLog通过融合深度概率编程与神经符号系统，突破了传统概率逻辑编程的局限性，为不确定性下的逻辑推理提供了更通用的解决方案。

authors_text: Jacqueline Ankenbauer,Parker C. Lusk,Annika Thomas,Jonathan P. How
title: Global Localization in Unstructured Environments using Semantic Object Maps Built from Various Viewpoints
title_zh: 使用从不同角度构建的语义对象图在非结构化环境中进行全局本地化
abstract: We present a novel framework for global localization and guided relocalization of a vehicle in an unstructured environment. Compared to existing methods, our pipeline does not rely on cues from urban fixtures (e.g., lane markings, buildings), nor does it make assumptions that require the vehicle to be navigating on a road network. Instead, we achieve localization in both urban and non-urban environments by robustly associating and registering the vehicle's local semantic object map with a compact semantic reference map, potentially built from other viewpoints, time periods, and/or modalities. Robustness to noise, outliers, and missing objects is achieved through our graph-based data association algorithm. Further, the guided relocalization capability of our pipeline mitigates drift inherent in odometry-based localization after the initial global localization. We evaluate our pipeline on two publicly-available, real-world datasets to demonstrate its effectiveness at global localization in both non-urban and urban environments. The Katwijk Beach Planetary Rover dataset is used to show our pipeline's ability to perform accurate global localization in unstructured environments. Demonstrations on the KITTI dataset achieve an average pose error of 3.8m across all 35 localization events on Sequence 00 when localizing in a reference map created from aerial images. Compared to existing works, our pipeline is more general because it can perform global localization in unstructured environments using maps built from different viewpoints.

Robustness to noise, outliers, and missing objects is achieved through our graph-based data association algorithm. Further, the guided relocalization capability of our pipeline mitigates drift inherent in odometry-based localization after the initial global localization. We evaluate our pipeline on two publicly-available, real-world datasets to demonstrate its effectiveness at global localization in both non-urban and urban environments. Demonstrations on the KITTI dataset achieve an average pose error of 3.8m across all 35 localization events on Sequence 00 when localizing in a reference map created from aerial images. Compared to existing works, our pipeline is more general because it can perform global localization in unstructured environments using maps built from different viewpoints.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种新颖的框架，用于在非结构化环境中进行车辆的全局定位和引导重新定位。与现有方法相比，我们的管道不依赖于城市固定装置（例如车道标记、建筑物）的线索，也不做出要求车辆在道路网络上导航的假设。相反，我们通过将车辆的本地语义对象图与紧凑的语义参考图（可能是从其他观点、时间段和/或模式构建的）稳健地关联和注册来实现城市和非城市环境中的定位。通过我们基于图的数据关联算法实现对噪声、异常值和缺失对象的鲁棒性。此外，我们的管道的引导重定位能力减轻了初始全局定位后基于里程计的定位中固有的漂移。我们在两个公开可用的真实数据集上评估我们的管道，以证明其在非城市和城市环境中全球定位的有效性。 Katwijk Beach Planetary Rover 数据集用于展示我们的管道在非结构化环境中执行准确的全球定位的能力。当在从航空图像创建的参考地图中进行定位时，KITTI 数据集上的演示在序列 00 上的所有 35 个定位事件中实现了 3.8m 的平均位姿误差。与现有的工作相比，我们的管道更加通用，因为它可以使用从不同角度构建的地图在非结构化环境中执行全局定位。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无需依赖城市固定设施的全局定位框架，通过多视角语义对象地图实现车辆在结构化与非结构化环境中的定位与再定位。
AI.motivation: 现有方法依赖车道线、建筑等城市设施或道路网络假设，难以适应非结构化环境。本文旨在构建更通用的定位方案，适用于城市与非城市场景。
AI.method: 基于图结构的数据关联算法，将车辆局部语义地图与多视角构建的紧凑语义参考地图进行鲁棒配准，并通过引导式再定位减少里程计漂移。
AI.result: 在Katwijk Beach和KITTI数据集测试中，KITTI数据集序列00的35次定位事件平均位姿误差为3.8米，验证了非结构化环境下的定位有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该框架通过多视角语义地图实现更通用的全局定位，相比现有方法在非结构化环境中表现更优。

search_text: Global Localization in Unstructured Environments using Semantic Object Maps Built from Various Viewpoints 使用从不同角度构建的语义对象图在非结构化环境中进行全局本地化 We present a novel framework for global localization and guided relocalization of a vehicle in an unstructured environment. Compared to existing methods, our pipeline does not rely on cues from urban fixtures (e.g., lane markings, buildings), nor does it make assumptions that require the vehicle to be navigating on a road network. Instead, we achieve localization in both urban and non-urban environments by robustly associating and registering the vehicle's local semantic object map with a compact semantic reference map, potentially built from other viewpoints, time periods, and/or modalities. Robustness to noise, outliers, and missing objects is achieved through our graph-based data association algorithm. Further, the guided relocalization capability of our pipeline mitigates drift inherent in odometry-based localization after the initial global localization. We evaluate our pipeline on two publicly-available, real-world datasets to demonstrate its effectiveness at global localization in both non-urban and urban environments. The Katwijk Beach Planetary Rover dataset is used to show our pipeline's ability to perform accurate global localization in unstructured environments. Demonstrations on the KITTI dataset achieve an average pose error of 3.8m across all 35 localization events on Sequence 00 when localizing in a reference map created from aerial images. Compared to existing works, our pipeline is more general because it can perform global localization in unstructured environments using maps built from different viewpoints. 我们提出了一种新颖的框架，用于在非结构化环境中进行车辆的全局定位和引导重新定位。与现有方法相比，我们的管道不依赖于城市固定装置（例如车道标记、建筑物）的线索，也不做出要求车辆在道路网络上导航的假设。相反，我们通过将车辆的本地语义对象图与紧凑的语义参考图（可能是从其他观点、时间段和/或模式构建的）稳健地关联和注册来实现城市和非城市环境中的定位。通过我们基于图的数据关联算法实现对噪声、异常值和缺失对象的鲁棒性。此外，我们的管道的引导重定位能力减轻了初始全局定位后基于里程计的定位中固有的漂移。我们在两个公开可用的真实数据集上评估我们的管道，以证明其在非城市和城市环境中全球定位的有效性。 Katwijk Beach Planetary Rover 数据集用于展示我们的管道在非结构化环境中执行准确的全球定位的能力。当在从航空图像创建的参考地图中进行定位时，KITTI 数据集上的演示在序列 00 上的所有 35 个定位事件中实现了 3.8m 的平均位姿误差。与现有的工作相比，我们的管道更加通用，因为它可以使用从不同角度构建的地图在非结构化环境中执行全局定位。 本文提出一种无需依赖城市固定设施的全局定位框架，通过多视角语义对象地图实现车辆在结构化与非结构化环境中的定位与再定位。 Jacqueline Ankenbauer,Parker C. Lusk,Annika Thomas,Jonathan P. How cs.RO

authors_text: Xinge Yang,Qiang Fu,Mohammed Elhoseiny,Wolfgang Heidrich
title: Aberration-Aware Depth-from-Focus
title_zh: 像差感知景深
comment: [ICCP & TPAMI 2023] Considering optical aberrations during network training can improve the generalizability
comments: [ICCP & TPAMI 2023] Considering optical aberrations during network training can improve the generalizability
abstract: Computer vision methods for depth estimation usually use simple camera models with idealized optics. For modern machine learning approaches, this creates an issue when attempting to train deep networks with simulated data, especially for focus-sensitive tasks like Depth-from-Focus. In this work, we investigate the domain gap caused by off-axis aberrations that will affect the decision of the best-focused frame in a focal stack. We then explore bridging this domain gap through aberration-aware training (AAT). Our approach involves a lightweight network that models lens aberrations at different positions and focus distances, which is then integrated into the conventional network training pipeline. We evaluate the generality of pretrained models on both synthetic and real-world data. Our experimental results demonstrate that the proposed AAT scheme can improve depth estimation accuracy without fine-tuning the model or modifying the network architecture.
summary: Computer vision methods for depth estimation usually use simple camera models with idealized optics. For modern machine learning approaches, this creates an issue when attempting to train deep networks with simulated data, especially for focus-sensitive tasks like Depth-from-Focus. In this work, we investigate the domain gap caused by off-axis aberrations that will affect the decision of the best-focused frame in a focal stack. We then explore bridging this domain gap through aberration-aware training (AAT). Our approach involves a lightweight network that models lens aberrations at different positions and focus distances, which is then integrated into the conventional network training pipeline. We evaluate the generality of pretrained models on both synthetic and real-world data. Our experimental results demonstrate that the proposed AAT scheme can improve depth estimation accuracy without fine-tuning the model or modifying the network architecture.
abstract_zh: 用于深度估计的计算机视觉方法通常使用具有理想光学器件的简单相机模型。 For modern machine learning approaches, this creates an issue when attempting to train deep networks with simulated data, especially for focus-sensitive tasks like Depth-from-Focus. In this work, we investigate the domain gap caused by off-axis aberrations that will affect the decision of the best-focused frame in a focal stack.然后，我们探索通过像差感知训练（AAT）来弥补这一领域差距。 Our approach involves a lightweight network that models lens aberrations at different positions and focus distances, which is then integrated into the conventional network training pipeline.我们评估了合成数据和真实数据上预训练模型的通用性。 Our experimental results demonstrate that the proposed AAT scheme can improve depth estimation accuracy without fine-tuning the model or modifying the network architecture.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种考虑镜头像差的深度从聚焦方法，以解决传统模型在模拟数据训练中的领域差距问题。
AI.motivation: 传统深度估计方法依赖理想化相机模型，难以处理真实场景中的镜头像差，导致模拟数据与实际数据间的领域差距，影响深度从聚焦任务的准确性。
AI.method: 提出像差感知训练（AAT）方法，通过轻量级网络建模镜头在不同位置和焦距下的像差，并将其整合到常规网络训练流程中，无需调整网络结构。
AI.result: 实验表明，AAT方法在合成数据和真实数据上均提升了深度估计精度，且无需模型微调或架构修改。
AI.conclusion: AAT方法有效缩小了模拟与真实数据间的领域差距，提升了深度估计性能，为实际应用提供了可行的解决方案。

authors_text: Guoliang He,Zak Singh,Eiko Yoneki
title: MCTS-GEB: Monte Carlo Tree Search is a Good E-graph Builder
title_zh: MCTS-GEB：蒙特卡罗树搜索是一个很好的电子图生成器
comment: Published in 3rd Workshop on Machine Learning and Systems (EuroMLSys 23), May 8, 2023, Rome, Italy
comments: Published in 3rd Workshop on Machine Learning and Systems (EuroMLSys 23), May 8, 2023, Rome, Italy
abstract: Rewrite systems [6, 10, 12] have been widely employing equality saturation [9], which is an optimisation methodology that uses a saturated e-graph to represent all possible sequences of rewrite simultaneously, and then extracts the optimal one. As such, optimal results can be achieved by avoiding the phase-ordering problem. However, we observe that when the e-graph is not saturated, it cannot represent all possible rewrite opportunities and therefore the phase-ordering problem is re-introduced during the construction phase of the e-graph. To address this problem, we propose MCTS-GEB, a domain-general rewrite system that applies reinforcement learning (RL) to e-graph construction. At its core, MCTS-GEB uses a Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) [3] to efficiently plan for the optimal e-graph construction, and therefore it can effectively eliminate the phase-ordering problem at the construction phase and achieve better performance within a reasonable time. Evaluation in two different domains shows MCTS-GEB can outperform the state-of-the-art rewrite systems by up to 49x, while the optimisation can generally take less than an hour, indicating MCTS-GEB is a promising building block for the future generation of rewrite systems.
summary: Rewrite systems [6, 10, 12] have been widely employing equality saturation [9], which is an optimisation methodology that uses a saturated e-graph to represent all possible sequences of rewrite simultaneously, and then extracts the optimal one. As such, optimal results can be achieved by avoiding the phase-ordering problem. However, we observe that when the e-graph is not saturated, it cannot represent all possible rewrite opportunities and therefore the phase-ordering problem is re-introduced during the construction phase of the e-graph. To address this problem, we propose MCTS-GEB, a domain-general rewrite system that applies reinforcement learning (RL) to e-graph construction. At its core, MCTS-GEB uses a Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) [3] to efficiently plan for the optimal e-graph construction, and therefore it can effectively eliminate the phase-ordering problem at the construction phase and achieve better performance within a reasonable time. Evaluation in two different domains shows MCTS-GEB can outperform the state-of-the-art rewrite systems by up to 49x, while the optimisation can generally take less than an hour, indicating MCTS-GEB is a promising building block for the future generation of rewrite systems.
abstract_zh: 重写系统[6,10,12]已广泛采用等式饱和[9]，这是一种优化方法，使用饱和电子图同时表示所有可能的重写序列，然后提取最佳序列。因此，可以通过避免相序问题来实现最佳结果。然而，我们观察到，当电子图未饱和时，它不能代表所有可能的重写机会，因此在电子图的构造阶段重新引入相序问题。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了 MCTS-GEB，一种将强化学习（RL）应用于电子图构建的领域通用重写系统。 MCTS-GEB的核心是使用蒙特卡罗树搜索（MCTS）[3]来有效地规划最佳的电子图构建，因此可以有效地消除构建阶段的相序问题，并在合理的时间内获得更好的性能。在两个不同领域的评估表明，MCTS-GEB 的性能比最先进的重写系统高出 49 倍，而优化通常只需不到一个小时，这表明 MCTS-GEB 是下一代重写系统的一个有前途的构建模块。

AI.tldr: 本文提出MCTS-GEB方法，通过结合蒙特卡洛树搜索（MCTS）与强化学习，解决e-graph构建中的阶段排序问题，提升重写系统的优化效率。
AI.motivation: 传统等价饱和方法在e-graph未饱和时无法表示所有重写机会，导致阶段排序问题重新出现，影响优化效果。
AI.method: 基于MCTS的强化学习框架，通过高效规划e-graph构建过程，消除阶段排序问题，实现更优的重写序列生成。
AI.result: 在两个不同领域测试中，MCTS-GEB性能较现有系统提升最高49倍，且优化时间通常低于1小时。
AI.conclusion: MCTS-GEB为未来重写系统提供了高效的构建模块，展示了其在解决阶段排序问题上的潜力。

ai_content.conclusion: MCTS-GEB为未来重写系统提供了高效的构建模块，展示了其在解决阶段排序问题上的潜力。

search_text: MCTS-GEB: Monte Carlo Tree Search is a Good E-graph Builder MCTS-GEB：蒙特卡罗树搜索是一个很好的电子图生成器 Rewrite systems [6, 10, 12] have been widely employing equality saturation [9], which is an optimisation methodology that uses a saturated e-graph to represent all possible sequences of rewrite simultaneously, and then extracts the optimal one. As such, optimal results can be achieved by avoiding the phase-ordering problem. However, we observe that when the e-graph is not saturated, it cannot represent all possible rewrite opportunities and therefore the phase-ordering problem is re-introduced during the construction phase of the e-graph. To address this problem, we propose MCTS-GEB, a domain-general rewrite system that applies reinforcement learning (RL) to e-graph construction. At its core, MCTS-GEB uses a Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) [3] to efficiently plan for the optimal e-graph construction, and therefore it can effectively eliminate the phase-ordering problem at the construction phase and achieve better performance within a reasonable time. Evaluation in two different domains shows MCTS-GEB can outperform the state-of-the-art rewrite systems by up to 49x, while the optimisation can generally take less than an hour, indicating MCTS-GEB is a promising building block for the future generation of rewrite systems. 重写系统[6,10,12]已广泛采用等式饱和[9]，这是一种优化方法，使用饱和电子图同时表示所有可能的重写序列，然后提取最佳序列。因此，可以通过避免相序问题来实现最佳结果。然而，我们观察到，当电子图未饱和时，它不能代表所有可能的重写机会，因此在电子图的构造阶段重新引入相序问题。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了 MCTS-GEB，一种将强化学习（RL）应用于电子图构建的领域通用重写系统。 MCTS-GEB的核心是使用蒙特卡罗树搜索（MCTS）[3]来有效地规划最佳的电子图构建，因此可以有效地消除构建阶段的相序问题，并在合理的时间内获得更好的性能。在两个不同领域的评估表明，MCTS-GEB 的性能比最先进的重写系统高出 49 倍，而优化通常只需不到一个小时，这表明 MCTS-GEB 是下一代重写系统的一个有前途的构建模块。 本文提出MCTS-GEB方法，通过结合蒙特卡洛树搜索（MCTS）与强化学习，解决e-graph构建中的阶段排序问题，提升重写系统的优化效率。 Guoliang He,Zak Singh,Eiko Yoneki cs.AI,cs.LG,cs.PL

authors_text: Renato Sortino,Simone Palazzo,Concetto Spampinato
title: Transformer-based Image Generation from Scene Graphs
title_zh: 从场景图生成基于 Transformer 的图像
abstract: Graph-structured scene descriptions can be efficiently used in generative models to control the composition of the generated image. Previous approaches are based on the combination of graph convolutional networks and adversarial methods for layout prediction and image generation, respectively. In this work, we show how employing multi-head attention to encode the graph information, as well as using a transformer-based model in the latent space for image generation can improve the quality of the sampled data, without the need to employ adversarial models with the subsequent advantage in terms of training stability. The proposed approach, specifically, is entirely based on transformer architectures both for encoding scene graphs into intermediate object layouts and for decoding these layouts into images, passing through a lower dimensional space learned by a vector-quantized variational autoencoder. Our approach shows an improved image quality with respect to state-of-the-art methods as well as a higher degree of diversity among multiple generations from the same scene graph. We evaluate our approach on three public datasets: Visual Genome, COCO, and CLEVR. We achieve an Inception Score of 13.7 and 12.8, and an FID of 52.3 and 60.3, on COCO and Visual Genome, respectively. We perform ablation studies on our contributions to assess the impact of each component. Code is available at https://github.com/perceivelab/trf-sg2im
summary: Graph-structured scene descriptions can be efficiently used in generative models to control the composition of the generated image. Previous approaches are based on the combination of graph convolutional networks and adversarial methods for layout prediction and image generation, respectively. In this work, we show how employing multi-head attention to encode the graph information, as well as using a transformer-based model in the latent space for image generation can improve the quality of the sampled data, without the need to employ adversarial models with the subsequent advantage in terms of training stability. The proposed approach, specifically, is entirely based on transformer architectures both for encoding scene graphs into intermediate object layouts and for decoding these layouts into images, passing through a lower dimensional space learned by a vector-quantized variational autoencoder. Our approach shows an improved image quality with respect to state-of-the-art methods as well as a higher degree of diversity among multiple generations from the same scene graph. We evaluate our approach on three public datasets: Visual Genome, COCO, and CLEVR. We achieve an Inception Score of 13.7 and 12.8, and an FID of 52.3 and 60.3, on COCO and Visual Genome, respectively. We perform ablation studies on our contributions to assess the impact of each component. Code is available at https://github.com/perceivelab/trf-sg2im
abstract_zh: 图结构场景描述可以有效地用于生成模型来控制生成图像的组成。以前的方法基于图卷积网络和对抗方法的组合，分别用于布局预测和图像生成。在这项工作中，我们展示了如何采用多头注意力对图信息进行编码，以及在潜在空间中使用基于变压器的模型进行图像生成可以提高采样数据的质量，而无需采用对抗模型，从而在训练稳定性方面具有后续优势。具体来说，所提出的方法完全基于变压器架构，用于将场景图编码为中间对象布局，并将这些布局解码为图像，通过矢量量化变分自动编码器学习的较低维空间。我们的方法显示了相对于最先进的方法改进的图像质量，以及同一场景图的多代之间更高程度的多样性。我们在三个公共数据集上评估我们的方法：Visual Genome、COCO 和 CLEVR。我们在 COCO 和 Visual Genome 上分别获得了 13.7 和 12.8 的 Inception Score，以及 52.3 和 60.3 的 FID。我们对我们的贡献进行消融研究，以评估每个组成部分的影响。代码可在 https://github.com/perceivelab/trf-sg2im 获取

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于Transformer架构的图像生成方法，通过场景图生成图像，无需对抗训练。
AI.motivation: 传统方法结合图卷积网络和对抗模型生成图像，存在训练不稳定问题。本文旨在通过Transformer提升生成质量并增强训练稳定性。
AI.method: 使用多头注意力编码场景图，通过Transformer在潜在空间生成图像，并结合向量量化变分自编码器（VQ-VAE）学习低维表示。
AI.result: 在COCO和Visual Genome数据集上分别达到Inception Score 13.7/12.8和FID 52.3/60.3，生成图像多样性优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: Transformer架构有效提升了图像生成质量与多样性，且无需对抗训练，实验验证了方法的有效性。

authors_text: Anthony Opipari,Jana Pavlasek,Chao Chen,Shoutian Wang,Karthik Desingh,Odest Chadwicke Jenkins
title: DNBP: Differentiable Nonparametric Belief Propagation
title_zh: DNBP：可微的非参数置信传播
comment: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2101.05948
comments: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2101.05948
abstract: We present a differentiable approach to learn the probabilistic factors used for inference by a nonparametric belief propagation algorithm. Existing nonparametric belief propagation methods rely on domain-specific features encoded in the probabilistic factors of a graphical model. In this work, we replace each crafted factor with a differentiable neural network enabling the factors to be learned using an efficient optimization routine from labeled data. By combining differentiable neural networks with an efficient belief propagation algorithm, our method learns to maintain a set of marginal posterior samples using end-to-end training. We evaluate our differentiable nonparametric belief propagation (DNBP) method on a set of articulated pose tracking tasks and compare performance with learned baselines. Results from these experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of using learned factors for tracking and suggest the practical advantage over hand-crafted approaches. The project webpage is available at: https://progress.eecs.umich.edu/projects/dnbp/ .
summary: We present a differentiable approach to learn the probabilistic factors used for inference by a nonparametric belief propagation algorithm. Existing nonparametric belief propagation methods rely on domain-specific features encoded in the probabilistic factors of a graphical model. In this work, we replace each crafted factor with a differentiable neural network enabling the factors to be learned using an efficient optimization routine from labeled data. By combining differentiable neural networks with an efficient belief propagation algorithm, our method learns to maintain a set of marginal posterior samples using end-to-end training. We evaluate our differentiable nonparametric belief propagation (DNBP) method on a set of articulated pose tracking tasks and compare performance with learned baselines. Results from these experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of using learned factors for tracking and suggest the practical advantage over hand-crafted approaches. The project webpage is available at: https://progress.eecs.umich.edu/projects/dnbp/ .
abstract_zh: We present a differentiable approach to learn the probabilistic factors used for inference by a nonparametric belief propagation algorithm. Existing nonparametric belief propagation methods rely on domain-specific features encoded in the probabilistic factors of a graphical model.在这项工作中，我们用可微分神经网络替换每个精心设计的因素，从而能够使用标记数据的有效优化例程来学习这些因素。通过将可微神经网络与有效的置信传播算法相结合，我们的方法学习使用端到端训练来维护一组边缘后验样本。我们在一组关节式姿势跟踪任务上评估我们的可微非参数置信传播（DNBP）方法，并将性能与学习的基线进行比较。这些实验的结果证明了使用学习因素进行跟踪的有效性，并表明了相对于手工方法的实际优势。 The project webpage is available at: https://progress.eecs.umich.edu/projects/dnbp/ .

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种可微分的非参数信念传播方法（DNBP），通过神经网络学习概率因子以提升姿态跟踪任务的推理性能。
AI.motivation: 传统非参数信念传播依赖手工设计的概率因子，难以适应复杂场景，而学习因子可提升模型灵活性和性能。
AI.method: 用可微分神经网络替代手工概率因子，结合信念传播算法进行端到端训练，学习维护边缘后验样本。
AI.result: 在姿态跟踪任务中，DNBP优于现有学习基线，验证了学习因子的有效性和实用优势。
AI.conclusion: DNBP方法通过自动学习概率因子，在姿态跟踪中表现出色，为非参数推理提供了新的高效解决方案。

authors_text: Furong Ye,Frank Neumann,Jacob de Nobel,Aneta Neumann,Thomas Bäck
title: Towards Self-adaptive Mutation in Evolutionary Multi-Objective Algorithms
title_zh: 进化多目标算法中的自适应变异
abstract: Parameter control has succeeded in accelerating the convergence process of evolutionary algorithms. While empirical and theoretical studies have shed light on the behavior of algorithms for single-objective optimization, little is known about how self-adaptation influences multi-objective evolutionary algorithms. In this work, we contribute (1) extensive experimental analysis of the Global Simple Evolutionary Multi-objective Algorithm (GSEMO) variants on classic problems, such as OneMinMax, LOTZ, COCZ, and (2) a novel version of GSEMO with self-adaptive mutation. To enable self-adaptation in GSEMO, we explore three self-adaptive mutation techniques from single-objective optimization and use various performance metrics, such as hypervolume and inverted generational distance, to guide the adaptation. Our experiments show that adapting the mutation rate based on single-objective optimization and hypervolume can speed up the convergence of GSEMO. Moreover, we propose a GSEMO with self-adaptive mutation, which considers optimizing for single objectives and adjusts the mutation rate for each solution individually. Our results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms the GSEMO with static mutation rates across all the tested problems. This work provides a comprehensive benchmarking study for MOEAs and complements existing theoretical runtime analysis. Our proposed algorithm addresses interesting issues for designing MOEAs for future practical applications.
summary: Parameter control has succeeded in accelerating the convergence process of evolutionary algorithms. While empirical and theoretical studies have shed light on the behavior of algorithms for single-objective optimization, little is known about how self-adaptation influences multi-objective evolutionary algorithms. In this work, we contribute (1) extensive experimental analysis of the Global Simple Evolutionary Multi-objective Algorithm (GSEMO) variants on classic problems, such as OneMinMax, LOTZ, COCZ, and (2) a novel version of GSEMO with self-adaptive mutation. To enable self-adaptation in GSEMO, we explore three self-adaptive mutation techniques from single-objective optimization and use various performance metrics, such as hypervolume and inverted generational distance, to guide the adaptation. Our experiments show that adapting the mutation rate based on single-objective optimization and hypervolume can speed up the convergence of GSEMO. Moreover, we propose a GSEMO with self-adaptive mutation, which considers optimizing for single objectives and adjusts the mutation rate for each solution individually. Our results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms the GSEMO with static mutation rates across all the tested problems. This work provides a comprehensive benchmarking study for MOEAs and complements existing theoretical runtime analysis. Our proposed algorithm addresses interesting issues for designing MOEAs for future practical applications.
abstract_zh: 参数控制成功地加速了进化算法的收敛过程。虽然实证和理论研究揭示了单目标优化算法的行为，但人们对自适应如何影响多目标进化算法知之甚少。在这项工作中，我们贡献了（1）对经典问题（例如 OneMinMax、LOTZ、COCZ）的全局简单进化多目标算法（GSEMO）变体的广泛实验分析，以及（2）具有自适应突变的 GSEMO 的新版本。为了实现 GSEMO 的自适应，我们从单目标优化中探索了三种自适应突变技术，并使用各种性能指标（例如超体积和倒代距离）来指导自适应。我们的实验表明，基于单目标优化和超体积调整突变率可以加快 GSEMO 的收敛速度。此外，我们提出了一种具有自适应突变的 GSEMO，它考虑针对单个目标进行优化，并单独调整每个解决方案的突变率。我们的结果表明，所提出的方法在所有测试问题上均优于具有静态突变率的 GSEMO。这项工作为 MOEA 提供了全面的基准测试研究，并补充了现有的理论运行时分析。我们提出的算法解决了为未来实际应用设计 MOEA 的有趣问题。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了多目标进化算法（MOEAs）中自适应突变机制的设计与优化，提出了一种基于单目标优化和超体积指标的自适应突变GSEMO算法。
AI.motivation: 尽管自适应参数控制在单目标进化算法中已被证明有效，但其在多目标场景下的影响尚不明确，需探索如何通过自适应突变提升MOEAs的收敛效率。
AI.method: 通过实验分析GSEMO算法在经典问题（如OneMinMax、LOTZ等）上的表现，引入三种单目标优化的自适应突变技术，并结合超体积等指标动态调整突变率。
AI.result: 基于单目标优化和超体积的自适应突变策略显著提升了GSEMO的收敛速度，新算法在所有测试问题上均优于静态突变率版本。
AI.conclusion: 该工作为MOEAs提供了系统性基准测试，补充了理论分析，并为未来实际应用中的MOEA设计提供了可行方案。

authors_text: Mélanie Lubrano,Yaëlle Bellahsen-Harrar,Rutger Fick,Cécile Badoual,Thomas Walter
title: Simple and Efficient Confidence Score for Grading Whole Slide Images
title_zh: 用于对整个幻灯片图像进行分级的简单高效的置信度评分
abstract: Grading precancerous lesions on whole slide images is a challenging task: the continuous space of morphological phenotypes makes clear-cut decisions between different grades often difficult, leading to low inter- and intra-rater agreements. More and more Artificial Intelligence (AI) algorithms are developed to help pathologists perform and standardize their diagnosis. However, those models can render their prediction without consideration of the ambiguity of the classes and can fail without notice which prevent their wider acceptance in a clinical context. In this paper, we propose a new score to measure the confidence of AI models in grading tasks. Our confidence score is specifically adapted to ordinal output variables, is versatile and does not require extra training or additional inferences nor particular architecture changes. Comparison to other popular techniques such as Monte Carlo Dropout and deep ensembles shows that our method provides state-of-the art results, while being simpler, more versatile and less computationally intensive. The score is also easily interpretable and consistent with real life hesitations of pathologists. We show that the score is capable of accurately identifying mispredicted slides and that accuracy for high confidence decisions is significantly higher than for low-confidence decisions (gap in AUC of 17.1% on the test set). We believe that the proposed confidence score could be leveraged by pathologists directly in their workflow and assist them on difficult tasks such as grading precancerous lesions.

abstract_zh: 在整个幻灯片图像上对癌前病变进行分级是一项具有挑战性的任务：形态表型的连续空间使得在不同等级之间做出明确的决定通常很困难，导致评估者之间和内部的一致性较低。越来越多的人工智能 (AI) 算法被开发出来帮助病理学家执行诊断并使其标准化。然而，这些模型可以在不考虑类别模糊性的情况下进行预测，并且可能会在没有通知的情况下失败，这阻碍了它们在临床环境中得到更广泛的接受。在本文中，我们提出了一个新的分数来衡量人工智能模型在评分任务中的置信度。我们的置信度分数专门适应序数输出变量，用途广泛，不需要额外的训练或额外的推论，也不需要特定的架构更改。与蒙特卡罗 Dropout 和深度集成等其他流行技术的比较表明，我们的方法提供了最先进的结果，同时更简单、更通用且计算强度更低。该分数也很容易解释，并且与病理学家现实生活中的犹豫一致。我们表明，该分数能够准确识别错误预测的幻灯片，并且高置信度决策的准确性显着高于低置信度决策（测试集上 AUC 差距为 17.1%）。我们相信病理学家可以直接在他们的工作流程中利用所提出的置信度评分，并帮助他们完成困难的任务，例如对癌前病变进行分级。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种简单高效的置信度评分方法，用于评估AI模型在全切片图像分级任务中的预测可靠性。
AI.motivation: 现有AI模型在病理分级中缺乏对类别模糊性的考量，导致预测结果不可靠且难以临床应用，需开发更有效的置信度评估方法。
AI.method: 设计适用于序数输出的置信度评分方法，无需额外训练或架构修改，通过对比Monte Carlo Dropout和深度集成等方法验证其有效性。
AI.result: 在测试集上实现17.1%的AUC差距，高置信度预测准确率显著高于低置信度，且计算效率更优。
AI.conclusion: 该置信度评分可直接融入病理工作流，辅助医生处理如癌前病变分级等复杂任务，提升诊断可靠性。
